name: pi
version: "0.1"
contract_version: agent-sync/v1
reserved_prefix: .pi

# concept_kinds is the authoritative per-IR-kind support declaration for the
# pi adapter. The in-code map in capabilities.go must match this file
# kind-for-kind; capabilities_test.go enforces the parity.
concept_kinds:
  agents-md:
    status: supported
    note: |
      Section-merged into the workspace-root AGENTS.md as a managed section
      between agent-sync:begin / agent-sync:end markers. Pi reads AGENTS.md
      (CLAUDE.md is also accepted) by walking from the repository root down to
      the cwd, and at user scope from ~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md. At user scope the
      adapter targets .pi/agent/AGENTS.md (→ ~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md).
  skill:
    status: supported
    note: |
      Emitted at .agents/skills/agent-sync-<id>/SKILL.md (plus assets). Pi scans
      the shared .agents/skills/ tree (parent-walked from cwd to repo root) — a
      cross-tool convention shared with codex, so one emitted skill tree serves
      both tools. codex+pi co-ownership of a leaf is made safe by the engine's
      union-aware drift/orphan checks (ADV-1). SKILL.md requires name +
      description frontmatter; Pi permits the skill name to differ from the
      folder name.
  command:
    status: unsupported
    note: |
      Pi supports prompt-template slash commands at .pi/prompts/<name>.md, but
      the agent-sync pi adapter does not emit them yet (planned). Pi's prompt
      directory is flat and shared with user prompts, so owning individual
      command files needs file-leaf swap support in the engine — tracked as a
      follow-up. Targeting command at pi surfaces a degradation warning today.
  mcp-server-entry:
    status: unsupported
    note: |
      Pi does not load MCP servers by design (a product position, not a gap) —
      see https://mariozechner.at/posts/2025-11-02-what-if-you-dont-need-mcp/.
      Targeting mcp-server-entry at pi surfaces a degradation warning and emits
      no files. To expose this capability to Pi, build or install a Pi extension
      that wraps it.
  rule:
    status: unsupported
    note: |
      Pi has no per-tool rule concept distinct from AGENTS.md. Targeting rule at
      pi surfaces a degradation warning and emits no files; consolidate rule
      content into an agents-md node or a skill instead.
  plugin-reference:
    status: unsupported
    note: |
      Pi packages/extensions install via `pi install` (npm/git) into settings,
      not from a project-level plugin-reference registry file. Targeting
      plugin-reference at pi surfaces a degradation warning and emits no files.

content.text: Pi covers agents-md + skills, but Antigravity needs the full set (rules, commands, MCP), so `claude` is the reference for those. Let me read claude's capabilities/emit/tool-owned code plus the registration and coverage sites.

content.content:
1	package claude
2	
3	import (
4		_ "embed"
5		"fmt"
6	
7		"github.com/goccy/go-yaml"
8	
9		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/internal/capmatrix"
10		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/internal/ir"
11		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/pkg/adapterkit"
12	)
13	
14	//go:embed capabilities.yaml
15	var capabilitiesYAML []byte
16	
17	// declarationFile is the parsed shape of capabilities.yaml. It is
18	// intentionally narrower than adapter.AdapterManifest so the file
19	// stays a per-adapter declaration and not an arbitrary manifest
20	// override.
21	type declarationFile struct {
22		Name            string                            `yaml:"name"`
23		Version         string                            `yaml:"version"`
24		ContractVersion string                            `yaml:"contract_version"`
25		ReservedPrefix  string                            `yaml:"reserved_prefix"`
26		ConceptKinds    map[string]conceptKindDeclaration `yaml:"concept_kinds"`
27	}
28	
29	type conceptKindDeclaration struct {
30		Status capmatrix.CapabilityStatus `yaml:"status"`
31		Note   string                     `yaml:"note,omitempty"`
32	}
33	
34	// conceptKinds is the in-code mirror of capabilities.yaml. The unit
35	// test asserts the two are kept in sync; if you add a kind here,
36	// add it there (and vice versa).
37	//
38	// The status values use capmatrix's vocabulary; the wire conversion
39	// happens in capabilitiesForWire.
40	var conceptKinds = map[ir.Kind]capmatrix.CapabilityStatus{
41		ir.KindAgentsMD:        capmatrix.Supported,
42		ir.KindRule:            capmatrix.Supported,
43		ir.KindSkill:           capmatrix.Supported,
44		ir.KindCommand:         capmatrix.Supported,
45		ir.KindMCPServerEntry:  capmatrix.Supported,
46		ir.KindPluginReference: capmatrix.Unsupported,
47	}
48	
49	// capabilitiesForWire returns the adapterkit.Capabilities the adapter
50	// echoes in its initialize response. WriteToolOwned is true because
51	// the claude adapter emits write_tool_owned ops for .mcp.json and
52	// CLAUDE.md.
53	func capabilitiesForWire() adapterkit.Capabilities {
54		builder := adapterkit.NewCapabilities().WithWriteToolOwned(true)
55		for kind, status := range conceptKinds {
56			switch status {
57			case capmatrix.Supported:
58				builder.Supports(string(kind))
59			case capmatrix.Partial:
60				builder.Partial(string(kind))
61			case capmatrix.Unsupported:
62				builder.Unsupported(string(kind))
63			}
64		}
65		return builder.Build()
66	}
67	
68	// declaredOutputs returns the list the adapter advertises in its
69	// initialize response. The runtime path-safety gate
70	// (internal/adapter/runtime.go pathInDeclaredOutputs) accepts an
71	// emitted op only when its path falls inside one of these declared
72	// outputs.
73	func declaredOutputs(scope string) []adapterkit.DeclaredOutput {
74		mcpPointer := "/mcpServers"
75		claudeMDSection := "agent-sync"
76		// The two tool-owned-entry paths and the sidecar are scope-resolved from
77		// the same source as the emit paths (emit_tool_owned.go resolvePathSet),
78		// so declared and emitted paths never drift — the path-safety gate
79		// (runtime.go pathInDeclaredOutputs) rejects any mismatch.
80		paths := resolvePathSet(scope)
81		outs := []adapterkit.DeclaredOutput{
82			{Path: ".claude/rules/agent-sync", Mode: adapterkit.OutputModeOwnedSubdir},
83			{Path: ".claude/commands/agent-sync", Mode: adapterkit.OutputModeOwnedSubdir},
84			// .claude/skills holds agent-sync-<id> leaf dirs alongside the user's own
85			// skills. shared-subdir → manage only our leaves, never the parent, so
86			// user skills survive a sync. (.claude/rules/agent-sync and
87			// .claude/commands/agent-sync are agent-sync-exclusive, so they stay
88			// owned-subdir and are swapped wholesale.) These already land under
89			// .claude/ and so are correct at user scope too — unchanged here.
90			{Path: ".claude/skills", Mode: adapterkit.OutputModeSharedSubdir},
91			{Path: paths.mcpJSON, Mode: adapterkit.OutputModeToolOwnedEntry, JSONPointer: &mcpPointer},
92			{Path: paths.claudeMD, Mode: adapterkit.OutputModeToolOwnedEntry, SectionID: &claudeMDSection},
93		}
94		// The strict-JSON sidecar marker is written next to an agent-sync-owned
95		// strict-JSON file at project/directory scope. At user scope the MCP target
96		// is Claude's own ~/.claude.json (paths.sidecar == ""), so it is neither
97		// declared nor emitted.
98		if paths.sidecar != "" {
99			outs = append(outs, adapterkit.DeclaredOutput{Path: paths.sidecar, Mode: adapterkit.OutputModeOwnedSubdir})
100		}
101		return outs
102	}
103	
104	// parseCapabilitiesYAML decodes the embedded YAML. Used by the parity
105	// test and by callers that want the human-readable note bodies.
106	func parseCapabilitiesYAML(src []byte) (declarationFile, error) {
107		var d declarationFile
108		if err := yaml.UnmarshalWithOptions(
109			src,
110			&d,
111			yaml.DisallowUnknownField(),
112			yaml.AllowFieldPrefixes("x-"),
113		); err != nil {
114			return declarationFile{}, fmt.Errorf("claude: parse capabilities.yaml: %s", yaml.FormatError(err, false, true))
115		}
116		return d, nil
117	}
118	
119	// loadDeclaration parses the embedded capabilities.yaml and returns
120	// the result. Cached at package level if it ever becomes a hot path;
121	// for now the call sites are test-only and one-off.
122	func loadDeclaration() (declarationFile, error) {
123		return parseCapabilitiesYAML(capabilitiesYAML)
124	}
125

1	package claude
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7	
8		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/pkg/adapterkit"
9	)
10	
11	const (
12		mcpJSONPath        = ".mcp.json"
13		mcpJSONPointerBase = "/mcpServers/agentsync_"
14		claudeMDPath       = "CLAUDE.md"
15		claudeMDSidecar    = ".agent-sync-managed"
16		sectionIDPrefix    = "agent-sync:"
17	
18		// scopeUser is the wire value (hierarchy.Level.String()) for the
19		// user-home scope. Any other value (incl. "" / "project" / "directory")
20		// is treated as project scope.
21		scopeUser = "user"
22	)
23	
24	// pathSet holds the scope-resolved destinations for the Claude adapter's two
25	// tool-owned-entry outputs and the strict-JSON sidecar. At project/directory
26	// scope they are workspace-root files (the historical behavior). At user
27	// scope they target Claude Code's real user-config paths — `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`
28	// and `~/.claude.json` (relative to the home scope root) — and the sidecar is
29	// suppressed, because `~/.claude.json` is Claude's own shared file, not an
30	// agent-sync-owned strict-JSON file the marker may claim.
31	type pathSet struct {
32		claudeMD string
33		mcpJSON  string
34		sidecar  string // "" ⇒ do not declare/emit the sidecar (user scope)
35	}
36	
37	// resolvePathSet maps the initialize scope to the adapter's tool-owned paths.
38	// Declared outputs (capabilities.go) and emitted op paths (here) MUST resolve
39	// from this one function so they never drift — a mismatch is rejected by the
40	// runtime's path-safety gate.
41	func resolvePathSet(scope string) pathSet {
42		if scope == scopeUser {
43			return pathSet{claudeMD: ".claude/CLAUDE.md", mcpJSON: ".claude.json", sidecar: ""}
44		}
45		return pathSet{claudeMD: claudeMDPath, mcpJSON: mcpJSONPath, sidecar: claudeMDSidecar}
46	}
47	
48	// markerOpenBytes is the literal HTML-comment opener every agent-sync
49	// section marker uses. Body content for an agents-md node is rejected
50	// when it contains this byte sequence: a hostile body could otherwise
51	// inject a fake end-marker followed by a fake begin-marker, splitting
52	// the managed section in CLAUDE.md and confusing the merge step.
53	var markerOpenBytes = []byte("<!-- agent-sync:")
54	
55	// emitMCPServerEntry emits one write_tool_owned op into .mcp.json
56	// at /mcpServers/agentsync_<id>, plus a sidecar .agent-sync-managed marker
57	// (deduplicated per emit) that advertises ownership next to the
58	// strict-JSON file.
59	//
60	// The body is required to parse as a JSON object. A scalar, array,
61	// or boolean body would corrupt the merged .mcp.json: Claude Code
62	// expects /mcpServers/<key> to map to an object with command/args
63	// fields, and storing a non-object value silently breaks every MCP
64	// load for the workspace.
65	//
66	// Validation is two-stage:
67	//  1. json.Valid — proves the bytes parse, catching string-encoded
68	//     bodies like "{" that look object-shaped at the first byte but
69	//     are actually unterminated JSON.
70	//  2. isJSONObject — proves the top-level structure is `{...}` and
71	//     not a number/array/bool/null that json.Valid alone would
72	//     accept.
73	func emitMCPServerEntry(emitted *emittedOps, node irNode, state *emitState) error {
74		body, err := decodeBodyOrPassthrough(node.Body)
75		if err != nil {
76			return wrapBodyErr(node, err)
77		}
78		if !json.Valid(body) {
79			return &adapterkit.Error{
80				Code:    adapterkit.CodeInvalidParams,
81				Message: fmt.Sprintf("claude: mcp-server-entry %q body is not valid JSON; refusing to corrupt %s", node.ID, state.paths.mcpJSON),
82			}
83		}
84		if !isJSONObject(body) {
85			return &adapterkit.Error{
86				Code:    adapterkit.CodeInvalidParams,
87				Message: fmt.Sprintf("claude: mcp-server-entry %q body must be a JSON object (got non-object)", node.ID),
88			}
89		}
90	
91		emitted.add(adapterkit.OpWriteToolOwned{
92			Path:    state.paths.mcpJSON,
93			Kind:    adapterkit.ToolOwnedKindJSONPointer,
94			Locator: mcpJSONPointerBase + node.ID,
95			Content: body,
96		})
97	
98		// The sidecar advertises agent-sync ownership next to an agent-sync-owned
99		// strict-JSON file. At user scope the MCP target is `~/.claude.json` —
100		// Claude's own shared config — so paths.sidecar is empty and we suppress it.
101		if state.paths.sidecar != "" && !state.sidecarEmitted {
102			state.sidecarEmitted = true
103			sidecar, err := adapterkit.NewOpWriteFile(state.paths.sidecar, 0o644, jsonSidecarMarker())
104			if err != nil {
105				return wrapOpErr(node, err)
106			}
107			emitted.add(sidecar)
108		}
109		return nil
110	}
111	
112	// emitAgentsMD writes the agents-md companion overlay into
113	// CLAUDE.md (workspace root) as a managed section between
114	// <!-- agent-sync:begin id=<id> --> and <!-- agent-sync:end id=<id> -->
115	// markers. CLAUDE.md is tool-owned by Claude Code; user content
116	// outside the managed section is preserved by the merge step
117	// (Unit 12a).
118	//
119	// The engine owns the begin/end markers — it renders the managed block
120	// from the locator during the markdown-section merge. The adapter passes
121	// the INNER body only; sending marker-wrapped content is rejected by the
122	// merge ("the engine owns markers; pass inner body only"). This mirrors
123	// the cursor and codex adapters.
124	//
125	// The body is still rejected when it contains the agent-sync marker
126	// opener. Without this guard a body containing
127	// `<!-- agent-sync:end id=other -->` could forge another section,
128	// leaving the merged CLAUDE.md with conflicting markers the merge step
129	// has no way to resolve safely.
130	//
131	// No managed-file header is added inside the section: the begin/end
132	// markers serve as the equivalent ownership advertisement, and a
133	// header inside a user-owned markdown file would be visually noisy.
134	func emitAgentsMD(emitted *emittedOps, node irNode, state *emitState) error {
135		body, err := decodeBodyOrPassthrough(node.Body)
136		if err != nil {
137			return wrapBodyErr(node, err)
138		}
139		if bytes.Contains(body, markerOpenBytes) {
140			return &adapterkit.Error{
141				Code:    adapterkit.CodeInvalidParams,
142				Message: fmt.Sprintf("claude: agents-md %q body contains agent-sync marker syntax (%q); refusing to corrupt CLAUDE.md", node.ID, string(markerOpenBytes)),
143			}
144		}
145	
146		emitted.add(adapterkit.OpWriteToolOwned{
147			Path:    state.paths.claudeMD,
148			Kind:    adapterkit.ToolOwnedKindMarkdownSection,
149			Locator: sectionIDPrefix + node.ID,
150			Content: body,
151		})
152		return nil
153	}
154	
155	// emitPluginReferenceWarning surfaces the unsupported declaration
156	// for a plugin-reference node. Claude Code does not load
157	// project-level plugin registries, so the adapter declines and
158	// surfaces a degradation warning the sync engine will report.
159	//
160	// No write_file is emitted. The runtime's capability-lied check
161	// (internal/adapter/runtime.go) only fires for kinds the adapter
162	// declared as `supported`, so emitting a warning-only result here
163	// is safe — plugin-reference is declared `unsupported` in
164	// capabilities.yaml.
165	func emitPluginReferenceWarning(emitted *emittedOps, node irNode) error {
166		emitted.add(adapterkit.OpWarning{
167			ConceptID: node.ID,
168			Status:    adapterkit.WarningStatusDegraded,
169			Note:      "Claude Code does not load project-level plugin-reference registries; install plugins via Claude Code's own plugin command",
170		})
171		return nil
172	}
173	
174	// isJSONObject reports whether body is a JSON object (`{...}`),
175	// not a scalar, array, or null. Assumes the caller already verified
176	// the bytes are valid JSON via decodeBodyOrPassthrough; the check
177	// here is purely structural — first non-whitespace byte must be `{`.
178	func isJSONObject(body []byte) bool {
179		for _, b := range body {
180			switch b {
181			case ' ', '\t', '\n', '\r':
182				continue
183			case '{':
184				return true
185			default:
186				return false
187			}
188		}
189		return false
190	}
191

1	package cli
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"fmt"
7		"time"
8	
9		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/internal/adapter"
10		claudeadapter "github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/internal/adapter/bundled/claude"
11		codexadapter "github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/internal/adapter/bundled/codex"
12		cursoradapter "github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/internal/adapter/bundled/cursor"
13		piadapter "github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/internal/adapter/bundled/pi"
14		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/internal/engine"
15		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/internal/fsroot"
16		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/internal/manifest"
17		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/internal/workspace"
18	)
19	
20	// bundledAdapters returns the compiled-in adapter set. Centralized so
21	// every command discovers the same adapters.
22	func bundledAdapters() []*adapter.BundledAdapter {
23		return []*adapter.BundledAdapter{
24			claudeadapter.Bundled(),
25			cursoradapter.Bundled(),
26			codexadapter.Bundled(),
27			piadapter.Bundled(),
28		}
29	}
30	
31	// prepared bundles the per-invocation engine inputs shared by sync and
32	// validate: an opened workspace root (the caller must Close it), the
33	// loaded manifest, and a fully-built engine.Request. Close releases the
34	// root.
35	type prepared struct {
36		Workspace *workspace.Workspace
37		Manifest  *manifest.Manifest
38		Root      *fsroot.Root
39		Request   engine.Request
40		Close     func()

1	// Package coverage reports when a scope emits a node kind that a target tool
2	// will not read natively at that scope's hierarchy level.
3	//
4	// agent-sync resolves precedence by emitting each scope to its own filesystem
5	// location and letting the target tool's native config hierarchy apply it
6	// (see the hierarchy-aware-manifests design). That only works as far as each
7	// tool actually reads a given kind at a given level. This package encodes the
8	// known native-read behavior per target and flags the gaps so users are not
9	// silently surprised by emitted content that never takes effect.
10	//
11	// The native-support table is keyed by target NAME and is static: a read-only
12	// analyzer cannot run an adapter Initialize handshake to learn declared
13	// outputs, and external adapters cannot be queried at all. Unknown targets and
14	// unknown kinds default to native (no false warnings). The directory- and
15	// user-level entries are the project's documented assumptions about each tool's
16	// read behavior; correct them here if a tool's behavior is verified to differ.
17	package coverage
18	
19	import (
20		"sort"
21	
22		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/internal/hierarchy"
23		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/internal/ir"
24	)
25	
26	// Warning is one (target, kind, level) combination that will be emitted but
27	// not read natively by the target tool at that level.
28	type Warning struct {
29		Target string
30		Kind   ir.Kind
31		Level  hierarchy.Level
32		Detail string
33	}
34	
35	// nativeAtDirectory[target] is the set of kinds the target reads natively from
36	// a NESTED directory. A kind absent from a target's set is non-native at the
37	// directory level and warns. The project level is always native (the tool reads
38	// its workspace-root config), so it is not represented here.
39	//
40	// Documented assumptions (verify against current tool behavior, correct here):
41	//   - claude reads nested CLAUDE.md (agents-md); it does NOT read rules,
42	//     commands, skills, or mcp entries from nested .claude/ directories.
43	//   - codex walks nested AGENTS.md (agents-md); nothing else nested.
44	//   - cursor reads nested .cursor/rules (rule); nothing else nested.
45	var nativeAtDirectory = map[string]map[ir.Kind]bool{
46		"claude": {ir.KindAgentsMD: true},
47		"codex":  {ir.KindAgentsMD: true},
48		"cursor": {ir.KindRule: true},
49	}
50	
51	// nonNativeAtUser[target] is the set of kinds the target does NOT read from any
52	// file-addressable user-global (home) location, so emitting them at user scope
53	// is inert. This is an inverted table from nativeAtDirectory: only targets with
54	// a user-scope gap appear, and an absent target/kind ⇒ native at user scope (no
55	// warning). Most tools read every supported kind from their user-global config
56	// (the user scope root is $HOME, so e.g. .codex/config.toml resolves to
57	// ~/.codex/config.toml), so they have no entry.
58	//
59	// Documented assumptions (verified against official docs 2026-06-30):
60	//   - cursor has no file-addressable user-global home for rules (User Rules
61	//     live in app settings / cloud, not a writable file) or AGENTS.md; only
62	//     ~/.cursor/mcp.json is file-addressable. So rule and agents-md are inert
63	//     at user scope.
64	//   - claude (scope-aware paths target ~/.claude/...) and codex (agents-md
65	//     remaps to ~/.codex/AGENTS.md; mcp + skills already resolve under $HOME)
66	//     read every supported kind from a user-global location → no entry.
67	var nonNativeAtUser = map[string]map[ir.Kind]bool{
68		"cursor": {ir.KindRule: true, ir.KindAgentsMD: true},
69	}
70	
71	// known reports whether we have a native-support table for target. Unknown
72	// targets default to fully native (no warnings).
73	func known(target string) bool {
74		_, ok := nativeAtDirectory[target]
75		return ok
76	}
77	
78	// Analyze returns the coverage warnings for emitting the given kinds to the
79	// given targets at the given level. Results are deterministically ordered by
80	// target then kind. The project level never warns (every tool reads its
81	// workspace-root config); the directory level warns for kinds not read from a
82	// nested dir, and the user level warns for kinds with no file-addressable
83	// user-global home. Targets with no table never warn.
84	func Analyze(level hierarchy.Level, kinds []ir.Kind, targets []string) []Warning {
85		var out []Warning
86		switch level {
87		case hierarchy.LevelDirectory:
88			for _, target := range targets {
89				if !known(target) {
90					continue
91				}
92				nativeKinds := nativeAtDirectory[target]
93				for _, k := range kinds {
94					if nativeKinds[k] {
95						continue
96					}
97					out = append(out, Warning{
98						Target: target,
99						Kind:   k,
100						Level:  level,
101						Detail: target + " does not read " + string(k) + " from a nested directory; this will not take effect until per-tool runtime mapping is added",
102					})
103				}
104			}
105		case hierarchy.LevelUser:
106			for _, target := range targets {
107				gap := nonNativeAtUser[target]
108				if gap == nil {
109					continue
110				}
111				for _, k := range kinds {
112					if !gap[k] {
113						continue
114					}
115					out = append(out, Warning{
116						Target: target,
117						Kind:   k,
118						Level:  level,
119						Detail: target + " has no user-global location for " + string(k) + "; emitted content is inert at user scope (sync it at project scope instead)",
120					})
121				}
122			}
123		default:
124			return nil
125		}
126		sort.Slice(out, func(i, j int) bool {
127			if out[i].Target != out[j].Target {
128				return out[i].Target < out[j].Target
129			}
130			return out[i].Kind < out[j].Kind
131		})
132		return out
133	}
134

1	// Package claude implements the bundled agent-sync adapter for Claude Code.
2	//
3	// The adapter consumes IR v1 nodes and emits the v1 op vocabulary
4	// (write_file, write_tool_owned, mkdir, delete, warning) for Claude
5	// Code's actual on-disk layout:
6	//
7	//   - rule    -> .claude/rules/agent-sync/<id>.md
8	//   - command -> .claude/commands/agent-sync/<id>.md
9	//   - skill   -> .claude/skills/agent-sync-<id>/SKILL.md plus assets
10	//   - mcp-server-entry -> .mcp.json (workspace root) via write_tool_owned
11	//     (user scope: .claude.json, i.e. ~/.claude.json)
12	//   - agents-md (companion overlay) -> CLAUDE.md (workspace root)
13	//     (user scope: .claude/CLAUDE.md, i.e. ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md)
14	//   - plugin-reference -> warning, unsupported by design
15	//
16	// The two tool-owned destinations are scope-aware: at user scope (sync --user)
17	// they target Claude Code's real user-config paths. See resolvePathSet.
18	//
19	// Bundled() returns an *adapter.BundledAdapter ready for the runtime
20	// to spawn over an in-process pipe; nothing else in this package is
21	// part of the public surface.
22	package claude
23	
24	import (
25		"context"
26		"fmt"
27		"io"
28	
29		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/internal/adapter"
30		"github.com/agent-sync/agent-sync/pkg/adapterkit"
31	)
32	
33	const (
34		adapterName    = "claude"
35		adapterVersion = "0.1"
36		reservedPrefix = ".claude"
37	)
38	
39	// Bundled returns the adapter.BundledAdapter for the claude target.
40	// The runtime registers it via adapter.DiscoverOptions.Bundled.
41	func Bundled() *adapter.BundledAdapter {
42		return &adapter.BundledAdapter{
43			Manifest: adapter.AdapterManifest{
44				Name:            adapterName,
45				Version:         adapterVersion,
46				ContractVersion: adapter.ContractVersionV1,
47				ReservedPrefix:  reservedPrefix,
48				// Bundled adapters are spawned in-process via Run; the
49				// Command slice is required by the shared manifest
50				// validator (validateAdapterManifest in
51				// internal/adapter/discover.go) but never executed for
52				// SourceBundled — the runtime takes the Run path. The
53				// placeholder name is recorded for diagnostics only.
54				Command: []string{"agent-sync-adapter-claude-bundled"},
55			},
56			Run: run,
57		}
58	}
59	
60	// run is the in-process entry point invoked by InprocTransport. It
61	// constructs an adapterkit.Server bound to the supplied pipes, wires
62	// up the lifecycle handlers, and serves until context is cancelled
63	// or the runtime sends shutdown.
64	func run(ctx context.Context, stdin io.Reader, stdout io.Writer) error {
65		server := adapterkit.NewServer(adapterkit.ServerOptions{
66			Name:    adapterName,
67			Version: adapterVersion,
68			Stdin:   stdin,
69			Stdout:  stdout,
70			// stderr defaults to os.Stderr; bundled in-process adapters
71			// share the parent process so this routes diagnostics to the
72			// CLI's own stderr, which is the documented place for them.
73			// Getenv defaults to os.Getenv; bundled adapters skip the
74			// magic-cookie check (the runtime sets cookie="" for bundled,
75			// see internal/adapter/runtime.go Initialize), so the env-var
76			// read still happens but the runtime ignores any echoed
77			// cookie value.
78			//
79			// We supply a constant 32-hex string for the env-var so the
80			// SDK's startup magic-cookie format check passes; the runtime
81			// ignores the echoed value for bundled adapters.
82			Getenv: bundledGetenv,
83		})
84	
85		// scope is captured at initialize and read at emit. The adapterkit Server
86		// processes initialize before emit on a single goroutine, so this needs no
87		// synchronization; each run() (one per session) has its own variable.
88		var scope, sourceURL, sourceCommit string
89	
90		server.OnInitialize(func(_ context.Context, params adapterkit.InitializeParams) (adapterkit.InitializeResult, error) {
91			scope = params.Scope
92			sourceURL = params.SourceURL
93			sourceCommit = params.SourceCommit
94			return adapterkit.InitializeResult{
95				Capabilities:    capabilitiesForWire(),
96				DeclaredOutputs: declaredOutputs(scope),
97			}, nil
98		})
99	
100		server.OnEmit(func(ctx context.Context, params adapterkit.EmitParams) (adapterkit.EmitResult, error) {
101			return handleEmit(ctx, params, scope, sourceURL, sourceCommit)
102		})
103	
104		if err := server.Run(ctx); err != nil {
105			return fmt.Errorf("claude bundled adapter: %w", err)
106		}
107		return nil
108	}
109	
110	// bundledCookie is a 32-hex placeholder sent through the adapterkit
111	// Server's cookie format check. Bundled adapters skip cookie
112	// validation at the runtime layer (see internal/adapter/runtime.go
113	// Initialize: cookie validation is gated on Source != SourceBundled),
114	// so the value is opaque to the runtime — only the SDK's "format
115	// must be 32 hex" check observes it.
116	const bundledCookie = "00000000000000000000000000000000"
117	
118	func bundledGetenv(name string) string {
119		if name == adapterkit.CookieEnvVar {
120			return bundledCookie
121		}
122		return ""
123	}
124	
125	// handleEmit lives in emit.go.
126

I have all the code-level facts I need. One key confirmation resolved a design wrinkle: **`ReservedPrefix` is a single string that does NOT need to cover every declared output** — `pi` sets `reserved_prefix: .pi` yet emits skills to the shared `.agents/skills/`. So Antigravity can own `.agent` as its reserved prefix while declaring `.agents/skills/` and `.agents/mcp_config.json` as separate shared/tool-owned outputs. The `.agent`/`.agents` split is cleanly expressible.

---
title: "feat: Add full-parity Antigravity bundled adapter"
status: active
date: 2026-07-03
type: feat
origin: docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md
---

# feat: Add full-parity Antigravity bundled adapter

## Summary

Add a new bundled adapter, `antigravity`, that translates agent-sync IR into
Google Antigravity 2.0's on-disk layout (IDE + CLI share the same config under
`~/.gemini/`). Antigravity replaced the retired Gemini CLI (2026-06-18) and reads
the same `GEMINI.md` overlay. The IR side is already wired — PR #36 routed the
root `GEMINI.md` overlay to the `antigravity` target in `internal/ir/decode.go`.
This plan builds the missing `internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/` package,
registers it, records its coverage behavior, and documents it.

The adapter is **full-parity**: it supports `agents-md`, `rule`, `skill`,
`command`, and `mcp-server-entry`, and declines `plugin-reference` with a warning
(matching `claude`/`pi`). `claude` is the structural reference (it is the only
existing full-parity adapter); `pi` is the reference for the shared `.agents/`
skills tree and scope-invariant capability set.

---

## Problem Frame

agent-sync renders team-standard agent guidance into each tool's native files.
Antigravity is a supported-tier target on the roadmap
(`docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md:57`) but has no
adapter — the only Antigravity presence today is the IR decoder's overlay-target
string. Users who author a canonical repo cannot currently sync to Antigravity.

The defining complication is that **Antigravity is internally inconsistent about
its config directory name**, and the adapter must reproduce this faithfully
rather than normalize it:

- **Rules and workflows** use `.agent/` (singular) — inherited from Antigravity's
  Windsurf/Codeium lineage.
- **Skills and MCP** use `.agents/` (plural) — the cross-tool AGENTS.md-ecosystem
  convention, shared with `codex`/`pi`.

This split is confirmed across Google's own codelabs (`.agents/skills/`,
`.agents/workflows` in the pipelines codelab) and Google DevRel posts
(`.agent/rules/`, `.agent/workflows/` — Mete Atamel, atamel.dev). See
**Sources & Research**.

---

## Requirements

- **R1** — New `antigravity` bundled adapter emitting the full IR v1 op vocabulary
  for Antigravity's real on-disk layout (see the mapping table below).
- **R2** — `agents-md` overlay targets **`GEMINI.md` only** (managed markdown
  section). Do **not** write `AGENTS.md` — that file is owned by `codex`/`pi` and
  a second writer at the same scope would collide. (Decision, see origin PR #36.)
- **R3** — Faithfully reproduce the `.agent/` (rules, workflows) vs `.agents/`
  (skills, mcp) directory split.
- **R4** — Scope-aware paths (project vs `--user`) resolved from a single
  `resolvePathSet`, so declared and emitted paths never drift (path-safety gate).
- **R5** — `capabilities.yaml` and the in-code `conceptKinds` map stay in parity
  (enforced by `capabilities_test.go`).
- **R6** — Registered in the single registration site and reflected in the
  coverage native-read table with honest user-scope gaps.
- **R7** — Passes `go vet ./... && go test -race ./... && golangci-lint run`.
- **R8** — Documented (`docs/adapters/antigravity.md`, README table, CHANGELOG)
  and the roadmap plan updated to mark the adapter landed.

---

## IR-kind → Antigravity path mapping (the core decision surface)

| IR kind | Project scope | User scope (`--user`, root = `$HOME`) | OutputMode | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `agents-md` | `GEMINI.md` | `.gemini/GEMINI.md` | `tool-owned-entry` (markdown section `agent-sync`) | GEMINI.md only; highest Antigravity precedence |
| `rule` | `.agent/rules/agent-sync/<id>.md` | *(no Antigravity global rules dir — inert)* | `owned-subdir` | singular `.agent`; agent-sync-exclusive subdir |
| `command` | `.agent/workflows/agent-sync/<id>.md` | *(global workflows live elsewhere — inert in v1)* | `owned-subdir` | singular `.agent`; Antigravity "workflows" = slash commands |
| `skill` | `.agents/skills/agent-sync-<id>/SKILL.md` (+assets) | `.gemini/skills/agent-sync-<id>/…` | `shared-subdir` | plural `.agents`; co-owned tree (codex/pi) |
| `mcp-server-entry` | `.agents/mcp_config.json` | `.gemini/config/mcp_config.json` | `tool-owned-entry` (JSON pointer `/mcpServers/agentsync_<id>`) | plural `.agents`; `{"mcpServers": {...}}` |
| `plugin-reference` | — | — | *(unsupported)* | Warning, matching claude/pi |

**Reserved prefix:** `.agent` (singular) — the Antigravity-exclusive native
directory. Skills and MCP live outside the reserved prefix (`.agents/…`),
declared separately as `shared-subdir` / `tool-owned-entry`. This mirrors `pi`,
whose reserved prefix is `.pi` yet emits skills to `.agents/skills`.

**User-scope resolution decision:** `agents-md`, `mcp-server-entry`, and `skill`
have genuine Antigravity user-global homes and are scope-aware. `rule` and
`command` have **no documented Antigravity global directory** (global rules fold
into `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md`; global workflows use a different path we are not
targeting in v1). Following agent-sync's existing honesty model (emit to our
location, let coverage flag non-native reads), `rule` and `command` keep their
project-relative `.agent/` paths at all scopes and are recorded in
`coverage.nonNativeAtUser` so `sync --user` warns they are inert. Capabilities do
**not** vary by scope (like `pi`); only paths and coverage do.

---

## Key Technical Decisions

- **KTD1 — GEMINI.md-only overlay.** Owning `GEMINI.md` gives Antigravity-specific,
  highest-precedence guidance and avoids fighting `codex`/`pi` over `AGENTS.md`.
  Confirmed with the user. (R2)
- **KTD2 — Faithful `.agent`/`.agents` split, not normalized.** The directory
  names are Antigravity's actual read paths; normalizing either would make emitted
  content inert. Expressed via distinct `DeclaredOutput` entries. (R3)
- **KTD3 — `claude` is the code template, `pi` the shared-tree reference.** Copy
  claude's file structure and emit dispatch wholesale (it is the only full-parity
  adapter), then take pi's scope-invariant `capabilitiesForWire` shape and its
  `.agents/skills` `shared-subdir` handling. Rename `adapterName`/`reservedPrefix`
  and rewrite `resolvePathSet` + `declaredOutputs` for Antigravity's paths.
- **KTD4 — Scope-awareness only where a user-global home exists.** agents-md, mcp,
  skill are scope-aware; rule, command are project-only + coverage-flagged. Keeps
  the adapter honest without inventing undocumented Antigravity paths. (R4)
- **KTD5 — MCP body validation reused verbatim.** Reuse claude's two-stage
  JSON-object validation (`json.Valid` + `isJSONObject`) before writing into
  `mcp_config.json` — a non-object body silently breaks every MCP load.

---

## Output Structure

```
internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/
  bundled.go              # Bundled(), run(), manifest, adapterName/reservedPrefix
  capabilities.go         # conceptKinds map, capabilitiesForWire, declaredOutputs
  capabilities.yaml       # embedded per-kind declaration (parity-tested)
  emit.go                 # handleEmit, dispatchNode, irNode wire shape, helpers
  emit_reserved.go        # emitRule, emitCommand, emitSkill (owned/shared subdirs)
  emit_tool_owned.go      # resolvePathSet, emitAgentsMD, emitMCPServerEntry
  emit_unsupported.go     # emitPluginReferenceWarning
  header.go               # managed-file header/marker rendering
  bundled_test.go
  capabilities_test.go
  emit_test.go
  header_test.go
  testdata/ir/
    agents-md-only.json
    rule-only.json
    skill-with-assets.json
    mcp-only.json
    mixed-everything.json
    plugin-reference-warn.json
docs/adapters/antigravity.md
```

---

## Implementation Units

### U1. Scaffold the antigravity package (manifest + lifecycle)

**Goal:** Stand up `bundled.go` with `Bundled()`, `run()`, the manifest, and the
`adapterName`/`adapterVersion`/`reservedPrefix` constants, wiring the
adapterkit lifecycle (`OnInitialize` → capabilities + declared outputs;
`OnEmit` → handleEmit).

**Requirements:** R1, R4
**Dependencies:** none
**Files:** `internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/bundled.go`
**Approach:** Copy `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/bundled.go` structure verbatim;
set `adapterName = "antigravity"`, `reservedPrefix = ".agent"`. Keep the bundled
magic-cookie/`bundledGetenv` scaffolding unchanged. Capture `scope`, `sourceURL`,
`sourceCommit` at initialize; pass to `handleEmit`. Package doc comment must state
the full kind→path mapping (mirror claude's doc block) including the
`.agent`/`.agents` split so the surprise is documented at the top of the file.
**Patterns to follow:** `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/bundled.go`.
**Test scenarios:** `Test expectation: none` here — behavior is covered by U2–U6
emit tests and U7 wiring tests. (Scaffolding unit.)
**Verification:** Package compiles; `Bundled()` returns a manifest with
`Name: "antigravity"`, `ContractVersion: v1`, non-empty `Command` placeholder.

### U2. Capabilities: conceptKinds map + capabilities.yaml (full parity)

**Goal:** Declare all five supported kinds + `plugin-reference` unsupported, in
both the in-code map and the embedded YAML, with accurate notes describing the
`.agent`/`.agents` split and the GEMINI.md-only overlay.

**Requirements:** R1, R2, R3, R5
**Dependencies:** U1
**Files:**
`internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/capabilities.go`,
`internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/capabilities.yaml`,
`internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/capabilities_test.go`
**Approach:** Mirror `claude/capabilities.go`. `conceptKinds`: `agents-md`,
`rule`, `skill`, `command`, `mcp-server-entry` = `Supported`; `plugin-reference` =
`Unsupported`. `capabilitiesForWire()` sets `WithWriteToolOwned(true)` (agents-md
+ mcp both emit `write_tool_owned`) and does not vary by scope (pi shape).
`declaredOutputs(scope)` calls `resolvePathSet` (U4) and returns:
`.agent/rules/agent-sync` (owned-subdir), `.agent/workflows/agent-sync`
(owned-subdir), `.agents/skills` (shared-subdir), `paths.mcpConfig`
(tool-owned-entry, JSON pointer `/mcpServers`), `paths.geminiMD` (tool-owned-entry,
section `agent-sync`). YAML notes must document each path and the singular/plural
split verbatim.
**Patterns to follow:** `claude/capabilities.go`, `pi/capabilities.yaml` (note
style), `pi/capabilities.go` (scope-invariant `capabilitiesForWire`).
**Test scenarios:**
- `TestCapabilitiesYAML_MatchesCodeMap` — YAML `concept_kinds` equals the in-code
  `conceptKinds` map kind-for-kind (copy claude's parity test).
- `TestCapabilitiesForWire_ExposesAllKinds` — wire capabilities list all five
  supported + plugin-reference unsupported; `WriteToolOwned` is true.
- `declaredOutputs` at project scope returns exactly the six declared outputs with
  the correct `OutputMode` and locator (JSON pointer for mcp, section id for
  agents-md).
- `declaredOutputs` at user scope returns `.gemini/GEMINI.md`,
  `.gemini/config/mcp_config.json`, `.gemini/skills` for the scope-aware three,
  and the unchanged `.agent/…` paths for rule/command.
**Verification:** Parity test green; declared outputs match the mapping table at
both scopes.

### U3. resolvePathSet + agents-md/mcp tool-owned emitters (scope-aware)

**Goal:** Implement scope resolution and the two `write_tool_owned` emitters
(GEMINI.md markdown section, mcp_config.json JSON pointer).

**Requirements:** R1, R2, R4, R5 (KTD5)
**Dependencies:** U1
**Files:** `internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/emit_tool_owned.go`
**Approach:** Define `pathSet{geminiMD, mcpConfig string}` and
`resolvePathSet(scope)`: user scope → `{.gemini/GEMINI.md, .gemini/config/mcp_config.json}`;
otherwise → `{GEMINI.md, .agents/mcp_config.json}`. `emitAgentsMD` emits
`OpWriteToolOwned{Kind: MarkdownSection, Locator: "agent-sync:"+id, Path: geminiMD}`
after rejecting bodies containing the marker opener (reuse claude's guard).
`emitMCPServerEntry` reuses claude's two-stage JSON-object validation and emits
`OpWriteToolOwned{Kind: JSONPointer, Locator: "/mcpServers/agentsync_"+id, Path: mcpConfig}`.
**No `.agent-sync-managed` sidecar** — Antigravity's `mcp_config.json` is a
tool-owned merge target, not an agent-sync-owned strict-JSON file, so the sidecar
(claude's strict-JSON ownership marker) does not apply. Confirm the merge engine
does not require it for JSON-pointer tool-owned entries (it does not for claude at
user scope, where the sidecar is likewise suppressed).
**Patterns to follow:** `claude/emit_tool_owned.go` (`resolvePathSet`,
`emitAgentsMD`, `emitMCPServerEntry`, `isJSONObject`, `markerOpenBytes`).
**Test scenarios:**
- agents-md node → one `write_tool_owned` at `GEMINI.md` (project) /
  `.gemini/GEMINI.md` (user), locator `agent-sync:<id>`, inner body only.
- agents-md body containing `<!-- agent-sync:` → `InvalidParams`, no op.
- mcp-server-entry with a JSON-object body → one `write_tool_owned` at
  `.agents/mcp_config.json` (project) / `.gemini/config/mcp_config.json` (user),
  locator `/mcpServers/agentsync_<id>`.
- mcp body that is valid JSON but not an object (array/number/string) →
  `InvalidParams`, no op.
- mcp body that is not valid JSON → `InvalidParams`, no op.
**Verification:** Tool-owned ops land at the correct scope-resolved paths; invalid
bodies fail closed.

### U4. Reserved/shared subdir emitters: rule, command, skill

**Goal:** Emit rules and workflows into agent-sync-owned subdirs under `.agent/`,
and skills into the shared `.agents/skills/` tree.

**Requirements:** R1, R3
**Dependencies:** U1
**Files:** `internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/emit_reserved.go`,
`internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/header.go`
**Approach:** Mirror claude's `emitRule`/`emitCommand`/`emitSkill` and its
per-subdir `mkdir` + `README.md` dedup (`readmeEmitted`) and per-path dedup
(`emittedFilePath`). Rewrite the destination roots: rule →
`.agent/rules/agent-sync/<id>.md`; command → `.agent/workflows/agent-sync/<id>.md`;
skill → `.agents/skills/agent-sync-<id>/SKILL.md` (+ assets under the leaf dir).
Skill leaf dirs are the only managed nodes inside the shared `.agents/skills`
parent (shared-subdir — never touch the parent or sibling leaves). Reuse claude's
managed-file header rendering (`header.go`) for the README/rule/command files.
**Patterns to follow:** `claude/emit_reserved.go`, `claude/header.go`,
`pi` skill emission into `.agents/skills`.
**Test scenarios:**
- rule node → `mkdir .agent/rules/agent-sync` + `README.md` + `<id>.md`
  (`write_file`), correct `OpRecord` order.
- command node → `mkdir .agent/workflows/agent-sync` + `README.md` + `<id>.md`.
- skill node with assets → `mkdir .agents/skills/agent-sync-<id>` + `SKILL.md` +
  each asset `write_file` at its `rel_path`; no op touches the `.agents/skills`
  parent.
- two skills → two independent leaf dirs, README dedup does not apply across
  skills (each leaf is self-contained), no duplicate `write_file` path error.
- asset `rel_path` colliding with `SKILL.md` → `InvalidParams` (path dedup).
**Verification:** Subdir ops land under the correct singular/plural roots; shared
tree parent is never emitted.

### U5. Emit dispatch + plugin-reference warning + wire plumbing

**Goal:** Wire `handleEmit`, `dispatchNode`, the `irNode` wire shape, dedup/sort,
node-targeting filter, and the unsupported `plugin-reference` warning.

**Requirements:** R1
**Dependencies:** U3, U4
**Files:** `internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/emit.go`,
`internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/emit_unsupported.go`
**Approach:** Copy claude's `emit.go` wholesale (irNode/irDocument shapes,
`emittedOps`, `records`, `wireOps`, `handleEmit`, `rejectDuplicateNodes`,
deterministic sort, `decodeBodyOrPassthrough`, `wrapBodyErr`/`wrapOpErr`,
`recordWritePath`). `nodeTargetsAntigravity` filters on `adapterName`.
`dispatchNode` routes rule/command/skill → U4 emitters, agents-md/mcp → U3
emitters, plugin-reference → `emitPluginReferenceWarning` (OpWarning,
`WarningStatusDegraded`, note: Antigravity has no project-level plugin-reference
registry). `emitState` drops claude's `sidecarEmitted` field (no sidecar).
**Patterns to follow:** `claude/emit.go`, `claude/emit_tool_owned.go`
(`emitPluginReferenceWarning`).
**Test scenarios:**
- `mixed-everything.json` (one of each supported kind) → the full expected
  `OpRecord` list in deterministic order.
- plugin-reference node → single `OpWarning`, status degraded, no file ops.
- node targeted at a different adapter (`targets: ["claude"]`) → silent skip.
- duplicate `(kind, id)` in payload → `InvalidParams`.
- unknown kind → `InvalidParams`.
**Verification:** `emitFixture` over each testdata doc yields the expected records;
mixed doc exercises every emitter.

### U6. Test fixtures

**Goal:** Provide the input-only IR JSON fixtures the emit tests read.

**Requirements:** R7
**Dependencies:** U2–U5 (co-developed with the tests referencing them)
**Files:** `internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/testdata/ir/*.json`
(`agents-md-only`, `rule-only`, `skill-with-assets`, `mcp-only`,
`mixed-everything`, `plugin-reference-warn`)
**Approach:** Adapt claude's / pi's fixtures. Keep node ids and bodies minimal but
realistic (mcp body a real `{"command": …}` object; skill with one asset).
**Patterns to follow:** `claude/testdata/ir/`, `pi/testdata/ir/`.
**Test scenarios:** `Test expectation: none` — fixtures are test inputs, exercised
by U2–U5.
**Verification:** All emit tests referencing these fixtures pass.

### U7. Register the adapter + coverage native-read entries

**Goal:** Compile the adapter into the bundled set and record its native-read
behavior so `sync` warns accurately.

**Requirements:** R6
**Dependencies:** U1–U5
**Files:** `internal/cli/setup.go`, `internal/coverage/coverage.go`
**Approach:** Add the import + `antigravityadapter.Bundled()` line to
`bundledAdapters()`. In `coverage.go`:
- `nativeAtDirectory["antigravity"]`: `{agents-md: true}` — Antigravity walks
  nested `GEMINI.md`/`AGENTS.md`; it does not read nested `.agent/rules`,
  `.agent/workflows`, `.agents/skills`, or `.agents/mcp_config.json`. (Verify the
  nested-read assumption for rules/workflows against current docs during
  implementation; default to non-native = warn if unconfirmed.)
- `nonNativeAtUser["antigravity"]`: `{rule: true, command: true}` — no Antigravity
  global directory for these (global rules fold into `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md`; global
  workflows use an untargeted path). agents-md, skill, mcp all resolve under
  `~/.gemini/…` so they are native at user scope (no entry).
**Patterns to follow:** existing `setup.go` slice, `coverage.go` claude/codex/
cursor entries + their documented-assumptions comments.
**Test scenarios:**
- Registration: a `bundledAdapters()`/discovery test (or existing adapter-count
  test) sees `antigravity` in the set. Check for an existing count assertion and
  update it.
- `coverage.Analyze(LevelDirectory, [rule, agents-md], ["antigravity"])` → one
  warning for `rule`, none for `agents-md`.
- `coverage.Analyze(LevelUser, [rule, command, skill], ["antigravity"])` → warnings
  for `rule` and `command`, none for `skill`.
**Verification:** `antigravity` is discoverable; coverage warnings match the
user-scope gap decision (KTD4).

### U8. Documentation + roadmap update

**Goal:** Document the adapter and mark it landed.

**Requirements:** R8
**Dependencies:** U1–U7
**Files:** `docs/adapters/antigravity.md`, `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`,
`docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md`
**Approach:** Write `docs/adapters/antigravity.md` mirroring
`docs/adapters/{claude,pi}.md`: the kind→path table, the `.agent`/`.agents` split
rationale with sources, GEMINI.md-only decision, user-scope coverage gaps. Update
the README planned-adapter table row (Antigravity: Planned → shipped/supported;
bump the bundled count "Four" → "Five" everywhere it appears). Add a CHANGELOG
entry. In the roadmap plan, update the deferred-adapter note (`:57`) to record the
Antigravity adapter as landed with the PR reference.
**Patterns to follow:** `docs/adapters/pi.md`, existing README table, CHANGELOG
stamping style (recent commits `docs: stamp CHANGELOG …`).
**Test scenarios:** `Test expectation: none` — docs. Verify the README bundled
count is consistent across all mentions (there is a count in the adapter table and
possibly prose).
**Verification:** Docs build/read cleanly; README count consistent; roadmap
reflects landed status.

---

## Scope Boundaries

**In scope:** the five supported kinds at project + user scope, coverage entries,
registration, docs.

### Deferred to Follow-Up Work

- **User-scope rule/command native homes.** If a future Antigravity release
  documents a global `.agent/rules`/workflows directory (or we decide to target
  `~/.gemini/antigravity/global_workflows/` for commands), make those kinds
  scope-aware and drop them from `nonNativeAtUser`.
- **Nested-directory runtime mapping.** Coverage warns that non-agents-md kinds are
  not read from nested dirs; per-tool nested runtime mapping is a separate,
  cross-adapter effort already noted in the coverage package.
- **`AGENTS.md` participation.** Antigravity also reads `AGENTS.md`; deliberately
  left to `codex`/`pi` to avoid multi-writer collision (KTD1). Revisit only if the
  overlay-ownership model changes.

### Non-goals

- Changing the IR decoder (already done in PR #36).
- Antigravity IDE-plugin or extension packaging (`plugin-reference` stays
  unsupported).

---

## Risks & Dependencies

- **Directory-name accuracy is the top risk.** The `.agent`/`.agents` split is
  sourced from Google codelabs + DevRel posts, not a single machine-readable spec
  (the official `antigravity.google/docs` pages are an SPA that resisted
  extraction). Mitigation: the mapping is documented with sources in
  `docs/adapters/antigravity.md`; if a path is later found wrong it is a localized
  change in `resolvePathSet`/`declaredOutputs`/`emit_reserved.go`. Confirm paths
  once more against a live Antigravity install before merge if one is available.
- **Workspace `mcp_config.json` path** (`.agents/mcp_config.json`) is
  single-sourced; the global path (`~/.gemini/config/mcp_config.json`) is
  well-corroborated. Low blast radius (one `resolvePathSet` line).
- **Adapter-count test drift.** Adding the fifth adapter may break an existing
  count assertion; U7 accounts for locating and updating it.
- **Dependency:** none external — IR wiring is already merged (PR #36).

---

## Sources & Research

- Antigravity MCP config (`~/.gemini/config/mcp_config.json`, workspace
  `.agents/mcp_config.json`, `{"mcpServers": …}`): Google Workspace MCP codelab;
  Composio "How to connect MCP servers with Google Antigravity".
- Skills `.agents/skills/`, `.agents/workflows/`, `agents.md`: Google codelab
  "Build Autonomous Developer Pipelines using agents.md and skills.md in
  Antigravity"; Gemini API "Antigravity Agent" doc (`.agents/skills/`).
- Rules/workflows `.agent/rules/`, `.agent/workflows/` (singular): Mete Atamel
  (Google DevRel), atamel.dev "Customize Google Antigravity with rules and
  workflows"; agentpedia.codes user-rules guide; zenn.dev Antigravity rules
  article.
- GEMINI.md vs AGENTS.md precedence + `AGENTS.md` added in v1.20.3 (2026-03-05),
  global `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md`: agentpedia.codes; gemini-cli issue #16058.
- Global skills `~/.gemini/skills/`: Medium (Dazbo) "Configuring MCP Servers and
  Skills for Antigravity CLI and IDE".
- Origin: `docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md:57`
  (deferred supported-tier `antigravity` adapter); `internal/ir/decode.go`
  (`GEMINI.md` → `antigravity` overlay target, PR #36).

---
title: "feat: Add full-parity Antigravity bundled adapter"
status: active
date: 2026-07-03
type: feat
origin: docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md
---

# feat: Add full-parity Antigravity bundled adapter

## Summary

Add a new bundled adapter, `antigravity`, that translates agent-sync IR into
Google Antigravity 2.0's on-disk layout (IDE + CLI share the same config under
`~/.gemini/`). Antigravity replaced the retired Gemini CLI (2026-06-18) and reads
the same `GEMINI.md` overlay. The IR side is already wired — PR #36 routed the
root `GEMINI.md` overlay to the `antigravity` target in `internal/ir/decode.go`.
This plan builds the missing `internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/` package,
registers it, records its coverage behavior, and documents it.

The adapter is **full-parity**: it supports `agents-md`, `rule`, `skill`,
`command`, and `mcp-server-entry`, and declines `plugin-reference` with a warning
(matching `claude`/`pi`). `claude` is the structural reference (it is the only
existing full-parity adapter); `pi` is the reference for the shared `.agents/`
skills tree and scope-invariant capability set.

---

## Problem Frame

agent-sync renders team-standard agent guidance into each tool's native files.
Antigravity is a supported-tier target on the roadmap
(`docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md:57`) but has no
adapter — the only Antigravity presence today is the IR decoder's overlay-target
string. Users who author a canonical repo cannot currently sync to Antigravity.

The defining complication is that **Antigravity is internally inconsistent about
its config directory name**, and the adapter must reproduce this faithfully
rather than normalize it:

- **Rules and workflows** use `.agent/` (singular) — inherited from Antigravity's
  Windsurf/Codeium lineage.
- **Skills and MCP** use `.agents/` (plural) — the cross-tool AGENTS.md-ecosystem
  convention, shared with `codex`/`pi`.

This split is confirmed across Google's own codelabs (`.agents/skills/`,
`.agents/workflows` in the pipelines codelab) and Google DevRel posts
(`.agent/rules/`, `.agent/workflows/` — Mete Atamel, atamel.dev). See
**Sources & Research**.

---

## Requirements

- **R1** — New `antigravity` bundled adapter emitting the full IR v1 op vocabulary
  for Antigravity's real on-disk layout (see the mapping table below).
- **R2** — `agents-md` overlay targets **`GEMINI.md` only** (managed markdown
  section). Do **not** write `AGENTS.md` — that file is owned by `codex`/`pi` and
  a second writer at the same scope would collide. (Decision, see origin PR #36.)
- **R3** — Faithfully reproduce the `.agent/` (rules, workflows) vs `.agents/`
  (skills, mcp) directory split.
- **R4** — Scope-aware paths (project vs `--user`) resolved from a single
  `resolvePathSet`, so declared and emitted paths never drift (path-safety gate).
- **R5** — `capabilities.yaml` and the in-code `conceptKinds` map stay in parity
  (enforced by `capabilities_test.go`).
- **R6** — Registered in the single registration site and reflected in the
  coverage native-read table with honest user-scope gaps.
- **R7** — Passes `go vet ./... && go test -race ./... && golangci-lint run`.
- **R8** — Documented (`docs/adapters/antigravity.md`, README table, CHANGELOG)
  and the roadmap plan updated to mark the adapter landed.

---

## IR-kind → Antigravity path mapping (the core decision surface)

| IR kind | Project scope | User scope (`--user`, root = `$HOME`) | OutputMode | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `agents-md` | `GEMINI.md` | `.gemini/GEMINI.md` | `tool-owned-entry` (markdown section `agent-sync`) | GEMINI.md only; highest Antigravity precedence |
| `rule` | `.agent/rules/agent-sync/<id>.md` | *(no Antigravity global rules dir — inert)* | `owned-subdir` | singular `.agent`; agent-sync-exclusive subdir |
| `command` | `.agent/workflows/agent-sync/<id>.md` | *(global workflows live elsewhere — inert in v1)* | `owned-subdir` | singular `.agent`; Antigravity "workflows" = slash commands |
| `skill` | `.agents/skills/agent-sync-<id>/SKILL.md` (+assets) | `.gemini/skills/agent-sync-<id>/…` | `shared-subdir` | plural `.agents`; co-owned tree (codex/pi) |
| `mcp-server-entry` | `.agents/mcp_config.json` | `.gemini/config/mcp_config.json` | `tool-owned-entry` (JSON pointer `/mcpServers/agentsync_<id>`) | plural `.agents`; `{"mcpServers": {...}}` |
| `plugin-reference` | — | — | *(unsupported)* | Warning, matching claude/pi |

**Reserved prefix:** `.agent` (singular) — the Antigravity-exclusive native
directory. Skills and MCP live outside the reserved prefix (`.agents/…`),
declared separately as `shared-subdir` / `tool-owned-entry`. This mirrors `pi`,
whose reserved prefix is `.pi` yet emits skills to `.agents/skills`.

**User-scope resolution decision:** `agents-md`, `mcp-server-entry`, and `skill`
have genuine Antigravity user-global homes and are scope-aware. `rule` and
`command` have **no documented Antigravity global directory** (global rules fold
into `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md`; global workflows use a different path we are not
targeting in v1). Following agent-sync's existing honesty model (emit to our
location, let coverage flag non-native reads), `rule` and `command` keep their
project-relative `.agent/` paths at all scopes and are recorded in
`coverage.nonNativeAtUser` so `sync --user` warns they are inert. Capabilities do
**not** vary by scope (like `pi`); only paths and coverage do.

---

## Key Technical Decisions

- **KTD1 — GEMINI.md-only overlay.** Owning `GEMINI.md` gives Antigravity-specific,
  highest-precedence guidance and avoids fighting `codex`/`pi` over `AGENTS.md`.
  Confirmed with the user. (R2)
- **KTD2 — Faithful `.agent`/`.agents` split, not normalized.** The directory
  names are Antigravity's actual read paths; normalizing either would make emitted
  content inert. Expressed via distinct `DeclaredOutput` entries. (R3)
- **KTD3 — `claude` is the code template, `pi` the shared-tree reference.** Copy
  claude's file structure and emit dispatch wholesale (it is the only full-parity
  adapter), then take pi's scope-invariant `capabilitiesForWire` shape and its
  `.agents/skills` `shared-subdir` handling. Rename `adapterName`/`reservedPrefix`
  and rewrite `resolvePathSet` + `declaredOutputs` for Antigravity's paths.
- **KTD4 — Scope-awareness only where a user-global home exists.** agents-md, mcp,
  skill are scope-aware; rule, command are project-only + coverage-flagged. Keeps
  the adapter honest without inventing undocumented Antigravity paths. (R4)
- **KTD5 — MCP body validation reused verbatim.** Reuse claude's two-stage
  JSON-object validation (`json.Valid` + `isJSONObject`) before writing into
  `mcp_config.json` — a non-object body silently breaks every MCP load.

---

## Output Structure

```
internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/
  bundled.go              # Bundled(), run(), manifest, adapterName/reservedPrefix
  capabilities.go         # conceptKinds map, capabilitiesForWire, declaredOutputs
  capabilities.yaml       # embedded per-kind declaration (parity-tested)
  emit.go                 # handleEmit, dispatchNode, irNode wire shape, helpers
  emit_reserved.go        # emitRule, emitCommand, emitSkill (owned/shared subdirs)
  emit_tool_owned.go      # resolvePathSet, emitAgentsMD, emitMCPServerEntry
  emit_unsupported.go     # emitPluginReferenceWarning
  header.go               # managed-file header/marker rendering
  bundled_test.go
  capabilities_test.go
  emit_test.go
  header_test.go
  testdata/ir/
    agents-md-only.json
    rule-only.json
    skill-with-assets.json
    mcp-only.json
    mixed-everything.json
    plugin-reference-warn.json
docs/adapters/antigravity.md
```

---

## Implementation Units

### U1. Scaffold the antigravity package (manifest + lifecycle)

**Goal:** Stand up `bundled.go` with `Bundled()`, `run()`, the manifest, and the
`adapterName`/`adapterVersion`/`reservedPrefix` constants, wiring the
adapterkit lifecycle (`OnInitialize` → capabilities + declared outputs;
`OnEmit` → handleEmit).

**Requirements:** R1, R4
**Dependencies:** none
**Files:** `internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/bundled.go`
**Approach:** Copy `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/bundled.go` structure verbatim;
set `adapterName = "antigravity"`, `reservedPrefix = ".agent"`. Keep the bundled
magic-cookie/`bundledGetenv` scaffolding unchanged. Capture `scope`, `sourceURL`,
`sourceCommit` at initialize; pass to `handleEmit`. Package doc comment must state
the full kind→path mapping (mirror claude's doc block) including the
`.agent`/`.agents` split so the surprise is documented at the top of the file.
**Patterns to follow:** `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/bundled.go`.
**Test scenarios:** `Test expectation: none` here — behavior is covered by U2–U6
emit tests and U7 wiring tests. (Scaffolding unit.)
**Verification:** Package compiles; `Bundled()` returns a manifest with
`Name: "antigravity"`, `ContractVersion: v1`, non-empty `Command` placeholder.

### U2. Capabilities: conceptKinds map + capabilities.yaml (full parity)

**Goal:** Declare all five supported kinds + `plugin-reference` unsupported, in
both the in-code map and the embedded YAML, with accurate notes describing the
`.agent`/`.agents` split and the GEMINI.md-only overlay.

**Requirements:** R1, R2, R3, R5
**Dependencies:** U1
**Files:**
`internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/capabilities.go`,
`internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/capabilities.yaml`,
`internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/capabilities_test.go`
**Approach:** Mirror `claude/capabilities.go`. `conceptKinds`: `agents-md`,
`rule`, `skill`, `command`, `mcp-server-entry` = `Supported`; `plugin-reference` =
`Unsupported`. `capabilitiesForWire()` sets `WithWriteToolOwned(true)` (agents-md
+ mcp both emit `write_tool_owned`) and does not vary by scope (pi shape).
`declaredOutputs(scope)` calls `resolvePathSet` (U4) and returns:
`.agent/rules/agent-sync` (owned-subdir), `.agent/workflows/agent-sync`
(owned-subdir), `.agents/skills` (shared-subdir), `paths.mcpConfig`
(tool-owned-entry, JSON pointer `/mcpServers`), `paths.geminiMD` (tool-owned-entry,
section `agent-sync`). YAML notes must document each path and the singular/plural
split verbatim.
**Patterns to follow:** `claude/capabilities.go`, `pi/capabilities.yaml` (note
style), `pi/capabilities.go` (scope-invariant `capabilitiesForWire`).
**Test scenarios:**
- `TestCapabilitiesYAML_MatchesCodeMap` — YAML `concept_kinds` equals the in-code
  `conceptKinds` map kind-for-kind (copy claude's parity test).
- `TestCapabilitiesForWire_ExposesAllKinds` — wire capabilities list all five
  supported + plugin-reference unsupported; `WriteToolOwned` is true.
- `declaredOutputs` at project scope returns exactly the six declared outputs with
  the correct `OutputMode` and locator (JSON pointer for mcp, section id for
  agents-md).
- `declaredOutputs` at user scope returns `.gemini/GEMINI.md`,
  `.gemini/config/mcp_config.json`, `.gemini/skills` for the scope-aware three,
  and the unchanged `.agent/…` paths for rule/command.
**Verification:** Parity test green; declared outputs match the mapping table at
both scopes.

### U3. resolvePathSet + agents-md/mcp tool-owned emitters (scope-aware)

**Goal:** Implement scope resolution and the two `write_tool_owned` emitters
(GEMINI.md markdown section, mcp_config.json JSON pointer).

**Requirements:** R1, R2, R4, R5 (KTD5)
**Dependencies:** U1
**Files:** `internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/emit_tool_owned.go`
**Approach:** Define `pathSet{geminiMD, mcpConfig string}` and
`resolvePathSet(scope)`: user scope → `{.gemini/GEMINI.md, .gemini/config/mcp_config.json}`;
otherwise → `{GEMINI.md, .agents/mcp_config.json}`. `emitAgentsMD` emits
`OpWriteToolOwned{Kind: MarkdownSection, Locator: "agent-sync:"+id, Path: geminiMD}`
after rejecting bodies containing the marker opener (reuse claude's guard).
`emitMCPServerEntry` reuses claude's two-stage JSON-object validation and emits
`OpWriteToolOwned{Kind: JSONPointer, Locator: "/mcpServers/agentsync_"+id, Path: mcpConfig}`.
**No `.agent-sync-managed` sidecar** — Antigravity's `mcp_config.json` is a
tool-owned merge target, not an agent-sync-owned strict-JSON file, so the sidecar
(claude's strict-JSON ownership marker) does not apply. Confirm the merge engine
does not require it for JSON-pointer tool-owned entries (it does not for claude at
user scope, where the sidecar is likewise suppressed).
**Patterns to follow:** `claude/emit_tool_owned.go` (`resolvePathSet`,
`emitAgentsMD`, `emitMCPServerEntry`, `isJSONObject`, `markerOpenBytes`).
**Test scenarios:**
- agents-md node → one `write_tool_owned` at `GEMINI.md` (project) /
  `.gemini/GEMINI.md` (user), locator `agent-sync:<id>`, inner body only.
- agents-md body containing `<!-- agent-sync:` → `InvalidParams`, no op.
- mcp-server-entry with a JSON-object body → one `write_tool_owned` at
  `.agents/mcp_config.json` (project) / `.gemini/config/mcp_config.json` (user),
  locator `/mcpServers/agentsync_<id>`.
- mcp body that is valid JSON but not an object (array/number/string) →
  `InvalidParams`, no op.
- mcp body that is not valid JSON → `InvalidParams`, no op.
**Verification:** Tool-owned ops land at the correct scope-resolved paths; invalid
bodies fail closed.

### U4. Reserved/shared subdir emitters: rule, command, skill

**Goal:** Emit rules and workflows into agent-sync-owned subdirs under `.agent/`,
and skills into the shared `.agents/skills/` tree.

**Requirements:** R1, R3
**Dependencies:** U1
**Files:** `internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/emit_reserved.go`,
`internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/header.go`
**Approach:** Mirror claude's `emitRule`/`emitCommand`/`emitSkill` and its
per-subdir `mkdir` + `README.md` dedup (`readmeEmitted`) and per-path dedup
(`emittedFilePath`). Rewrite the destination roots: rule →
`.agent/rules/agent-sync/<id>.md`; command → `.agent/workflows/agent-sync/<id>.md`;
skill → `.agents/skills/agent-sync-<id>/SKILL.md` (+ assets under the leaf dir).
Skill leaf dirs are the only managed nodes inside the shared `.agents/skills`
parent (shared-subdir — never touch the parent or sibling leaves). Reuse claude's
managed-file header rendering (`header.go`) for the README/rule/command files.
**Patterns to follow:** `claude/emit_reserved.go`, `claude/header.go`,
`pi` skill emission into `.agents/skills`.
**Test scenarios:**
- rule node → `mkdir .agent/rules/agent-sync` + `README.md` + `<id>.md`
  (`write_file`), correct `OpRecord` order.
- command node → `mkdir .agent/workflows/agent-sync` + `README.md` + `<id>.md`.
- skill node with assets → `mkdir .agents/skills/agent-sync-<id>` + `SKILL.md` +
  each asset `write_file` at its `rel_path`; no op touches the `.agents/skills`
  parent.
- two skills → two independent leaf dirs, README dedup does not apply across
  skills (each leaf is self-contained), no duplicate `write_file` path error.
- asset `rel_path` colliding with `SKILL.md` → `InvalidParams` (path dedup).
**Verification:** Subdir ops land under the correct singular/plural roots; shared
tree parent is never emitted.

### U5. Emit dispatch + plugin-reference warning + wire plumbing

**Goal:** Wire `handleEmit`, `dispatchNode`, the `irNode` wire shape, dedup/sort,
node-targeting filter, and the unsupported `plugin-reference` warning.

**Requirements:** R1
**Dependencies:** U3, U4
**Files:** `internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/emit.go`,
`internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/emit_unsupported.go`
**Approach:** Copy claude's `emit.go` wholesale (irNode/irDocument shapes,
`emittedOps`, `records`, `wireOps`, `handleEmit`, `rejectDuplicateNodes`,
deterministic sort, `decodeBodyOrPassthrough`, `wrapBodyErr`/`wrapOpErr`,
`recordWritePath`). `nodeTargetsAntigravity` filters on `adapterName`.
`dispatchNode` routes rule/command/skill → U4 emitters, agents-md/mcp → U3
emitters, plugin-reference → `emitPluginReferenceWarning` (OpWarning,
`WarningStatusDegraded`, note: Antigravity has no project-level plugin-reference
registry). `emitState` drops claude's `sidecarEmitted` field (no sidecar).
**Patterns to follow:** `claude/emit.go`, `claude/emit_tool_owned.go`
(`emitPluginReferenceWarning`).
**Test scenarios:**
- `mixed-everything.json` (one of each supported kind) → the full expected
  `OpRecord` list in deterministic order.
- plugin-reference node → single `OpWarning`, status degraded, no file ops.
- node targeted at a different adapter (`targets: ["claude"]`) → silent skip.
- duplicate `(kind, id)` in payload → `InvalidParams`.
- unknown kind → `InvalidParams`.
**Verification:** `emitFixture` over each testdata doc yields the expected records;
mixed doc exercises every emitter.

### U6. Test fixtures

**Goal:** Provide the input-only IR JSON fixtures the emit tests read.

**Requirements:** R7
**Dependencies:** U2–U5 (co-developed with the tests referencing them)
**Files:** `internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/testdata/ir/*.json`
(`agents-md-only`, `rule-only`, `skill-with-assets`, `mcp-only`,
`mixed-everything`, `plugin-reference-warn`)
**Approach:** Adapt claude's / pi's fixtures. Keep node ids and bodies minimal but
realistic (mcp body a real `{"command": …}` object; skill with one asset).
**Patterns to follow:** `claude/testdata/ir/`, `pi/testdata/ir/`.
**Test scenarios:** `Test expectation: none` — fixtures are test inputs, exercised
by U2–U5.
**Verification:** All emit tests referencing these fixtures pass.

### U7. Register the adapter + coverage native-read entries

**Goal:** Compile the adapter into the bundled set and record its native-read
behavior so `sync` warns accurately.

**Requirements:** R6
**Dependencies:** U1–U5
**Files:** `internal/cli/setup.go`, `internal/coverage/coverage.go`
**Approach:** Add the import + `antigravityadapter.Bundled()` line to
`bundledAdapters()`. In `coverage.go`:
- `nativeAtDirectory["antigravity"]`: `{agents-md: true}` — Antigravity walks
  nested `GEMINI.md`/`AGENTS.md`; it does not read nested `.agent/rules`,
  `.agent/workflows`, `.agents/skills`, or `.agents/mcp_config.json`. (Verify the
  nested-read assumption for rules/workflows against current docs during
  implementation; default to non-native = warn if unconfirmed.)
- `nonNativeAtUser["antigravity"]`: `{rule: true, command: true}` — no Antigravity
  global directory for these (global rules fold into `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md`; global
  workflows use an untargeted path). agents-md, skill, mcp all resolve under
  `~/.gemini/…` so they are native at user scope (no entry).
**Patterns to follow:** existing `setup.go` slice, `coverage.go` claude/codex/
cursor entries + their documented-assumptions comments.
**Test scenarios:**
- Registration: a `bundledAdapters()`/discovery test (or existing adapter-count
  test) sees `antigravity` in the set. Check for an existing count assertion and
  update it.
- `coverage.Analyze(LevelDirectory, [rule, agents-md], ["antigravity"])` → one
  warning for `rule`, none for `agents-md`.
- `coverage.Analyze(LevelUser, [rule, command, skill], ["antigravity"])` → warnings
  for `rule` and `command`, none for `skill`.
**Verification:** `antigravity` is discoverable; coverage warnings match the
user-scope gap decision (KTD4).

### U8. Documentation + roadmap update

**Goal:** Document the adapter and mark it landed.

**Requirements:** R8
**Dependencies:** U1–U7
**Files:** `docs/adapters/antigravity.md`, `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`,
`docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md`
**Approach:** Write `docs/adapters/antigravity.md` mirroring
`docs/adapters/{claude,pi}.md`: the kind→path table, the `.agent`/`.agents` split
rationale with sources, GEMINI.md-only decision, user-scope coverage gaps. Update
the README planned-adapter table row (Antigravity: Planned → shipped/supported;
bump the bundled count "Four" → "Five" everywhere it appears). Add a CHANGELOG
entry. In the roadmap plan, update the deferred-adapter note (`:57`) to record the
Antigravity adapter as landed with the PR reference.
**Patterns to follow:** `docs/adapters/pi.md`, existing README table, CHANGELOG
stamping style (recent commits `docs: stamp CHANGELOG …`).
**Test scenarios:** `Test expectation: none` — docs. Verify the README bundled
count is consistent across all mentions (there is a count in the adapter table and
possibly prose).
**Verification:** Docs build/read cleanly; README count consistent; roadmap
reflects landed status.

---

## Scope Boundaries

**In scope:** the five supported kinds at project + user scope, coverage entries,
registration, docs.

### Deferred to Follow-Up Work

- **User-scope rule/command native homes.** If a future Antigravity release
  documents a global `.agent/rules`/workflows directory (or we decide to target
  `~/.gemini/antigravity/global_workflows/` for commands), make those kinds
  scope-aware and drop them from `nonNativeAtUser`.
- **Nested-directory runtime mapping.** Coverage warns that non-agents-md kinds are
  not read from nested dirs; per-tool nested runtime mapping is a separate,
  cross-adapter effort already noted in the coverage package.
- **`AGENTS.md` participation.** Antigravity also reads `AGENTS.md`; deliberately
  left to `codex`/`pi` to avoid multi-writer collision (KTD1). Revisit only if the
  overlay-ownership model changes.

### Non-goals

- Changing the IR decoder (already done in PR #36).
- Antigravity IDE-plugin or extension packaging (`plugin-reference` stays
  unsupported).

---

## Risks & Dependencies

- **Directory-name accuracy is the top risk.** The `.agent`/`.agents` split is
  sourced from Google codelabs + DevRel posts, not a single machine-readable spec
  (the official `antigravity.google/docs` pages are an SPA that resisted
  extraction). Mitigation: the mapping is documented with sources in
  `docs/adapters/antigravity.md`; if a path is later found wrong it is a localized
  change in `resolvePathSet`/`declaredOutputs`/`emit_reserved.go`. Confirm paths
  once more against a live Antigravity install before merge if one is available.
- **Workspace `mcp_config.json` path** (`.agents/mcp_config.json`) is
  single-sourced; the global path (`~/.gemini/config/mcp_config.json`) is
  well-corroborated. Low blast radius (one `resolvePathSet` line).
- **Adapter-count test drift.** Adding the fifth adapter may break an existing
  count assertion; U7 accounts for locating and updating it.
- **Dependency:** none external — IR wiring is already merged (PR #36).

---

## Sources & Research

- Antigravity MCP config (`~/.gemini/config/mcp_config.json`, workspace
  `.agents/mcp_config.json`, `{"mcpServers": …}`): Google Workspace MCP codelab;
  Composio "How to connect MCP servers with Google Antigravity".
- Skills `.agents/skills/`, `.agents/workflows/`, `agents.md`: Google codelab
  "Build Autonomous Developer Pipelines using agents.md and skills.md in
  Antigravity"; Gemini API "Antigravity Agent" doc (`.agents/skills/`).
- Rules/workflows `.agent/rules/`, `.agent/workflows/` (singular): Mete Atamel
  (Google DevRel), atamel.dev "Customize Google Antigravity with rules and
  workflows"; agentpedia.codes user-rules guide; zenn.dev Antigravity rules
  article.
- GEMINI.md vs AGENTS.md precedence + `AGENTS.md` added in v1.20.3 (2026-03-05),
  global `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md`: agentpedia.codes; gemini-cli issue #16058.
- Global skills `~/.gemini/skills/`: Medium (Dazbo) "Configuring MCP Servers and
  Skills for Antigravity CLI and IDE".
- Origin: `docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md:57`
  (deferred supported-tier `antigravity` adapter); `internal/ir/decode.go`
  (`GEMINI.md` → `antigravity` overlay target, PR #36).

aiTitle: Add Antigravity support with v2 API

content.text:
Plan written to `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/docs/plans/2026-07-03-001-feat-antigravity-adapter-plan.md`.

## What the research established

**"v2" = Antigravity 2.0** (IDE + CLI, config under `~/.gemini/`). The IR side is already done — PR #36 routed `GEMINI.md` → the `antigravity` target. What's missing is the adapter package itself.

**The load-bearing finding:** Antigravity is genuinely inconsistent about its config directory name, and the adapter must reproduce it faithfully:
- **Rules & workflows → `.agent/`** (singular, Windsurf lineage)
- **Skills & MCP → `.agents/`** (plural, cross-tool AGENTS.md convention)

This is confirmed across Google's own codelabs and a Google DevRel post (sources cited in the plan). I verified against the code that this split is cleanly expressible — `reserved_prefix` is a single string that needn't cover every output (pi proves it: `.pi` prefix, but skills go to `.agents/skills`).

## The plan (8 units, Standard depth)

`claude` is the code template (only full-parity adapter); `pi` is the reference for the shared `.agents/skills` tree. Full IR-kind → path mapping, both scopes, is tabulated in the plan. Key decisions locked per your answers: **GEMINI.md-only overlay** (no AGENTS.md collision with codex/pi), **full parity**. Rule/command are project-scope-only with honest `nonNativeAtUser` coverage warnings (Antigravity has no documented global dir for them).

The one residual risk I flagged: the `.agent`/`.agents` paths come from codelabs + DevRel posts, not a machine-readable spec (the official docs site is an SPA that resisted extraction). It's a localized change in `resolvePathSet` if anything's off — worth a final check against a live install before merge if you have one.
