toolUseResult.originalFile:
# agent-sync

[![Release](https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync?label=release)](https://github.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync/releases/latest)

`agent-sync` is a Go CLI that keeps AI-agent configuration for multiple tools
(Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI — and more to come) in sync from a single
Git-backed manifest. Think of it as `dotfiles` for agents: one canonical
source of skills, rules, commands, and MCP servers; native files rendered
into each tool's own conventions.

> **Status:** pre-alpha. v1 is in active development. Architecture and
> rationale live in the [requirements](docs/brainstorms/2026-04-21-aienvs-agent-workspace-requirements.md)
> and [implementation plan](docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md).

## Install

Download the prebuilt binary for your platform, verify its checksum, and put it
on your PATH — one command:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync/main/install.sh | sh
```

Installs to `/usr/local/bin` (falling back to `~/.local/bin` if that isn't
writable). Pin a version or change the location with env vars:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync/main/install.sh \
  | AGENT_SYNC_VERSION=v0.1.0 AGENT_SYNC_INSTALL_DIR="$HOME/bin" sh
```

Prefer not to pipe to a shell? Download the archive for your OS/arch from the
[Releases page](https://github.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync/releases), verify it
against `checksums.txt`, extract the `agent-sync` binary, and move it onto your
PATH. (Windows: grab the `.zip`.) Git must be on your PATH at runtime.

## Quickstart

New to agent-sync? [`docs/quickstart.md`](docs/quickstart.md) walks through
authoring a canonical repo and running your first `init → sync → validate`,
with a copyable example under [`examples/canonical/`](examples/canonical/).

## What agent-sync does

1. **Manifests form a hierarchy.** A `.agent-sync.yaml` binds a directory to a
   canonical source and declares which target tools receive configuration. The
   source is one of: a remote Git repo (`url`) or a local clone (`local_path`),
   both pinned by commit SHA; or an in-repo working-tree directory (`local_dir`,
   e.g. `.agents`) for per-repo skills authored right in the repo — unpinned,
   and exempt from trust and offline-strict since there's nothing remote to fetch.
   `sync` walks up from the current directory (project root = nearest `.git`
   ancestor) and emits every manifest it finds, each to its own scope; the
   user-level manifest at `~` is emitted only with `sync --user`. agent-sync
   never merges across levels — each target tool resolves precedence via its own
   native config hierarchy, and `sync` warns when a scope emits a kind a tool
   won't read natively at that level.
2. **Explicit `sync`.** `agent-sync sync` materializes the pinned content,
   compiles it via per-tool adapters, stages the output, and atomically
   swaps it into each tool's reserved subdirectory (e.g.
   `.claude/rules/agent-sync/`, `.cursor/rules/agent-sync/`, `.codex/skills/agent-sync-<id>/`).
   Emitted skills carry a real `name` / `description` frontmatter block so
   they show up correctly in each tool's skill list, and every managed file
   records its source (`<url>@<short-sha>`) in its header.
3. **Moving the pin: `update`.** `agent-sync update` fetches the canonical
   remote, shows what changed since the current pin, and — on confirmation
   (or `--accept-update=<sha>` when non-interactive) — re-pins
   `commit` + `trusted_sha` and re-syncs in one locked step. It is
   fast-forward-only: a rewritten upstream history is refused unless you
   pass `--accept-rewritten-history=<sha>`. Use `--user` to update the
   `~` manifest.
4. **Safe by default.** Pinning, offline-strict, TOFU on `(URL, SHA)`
   pairs, reserved-prefix ownership with a ledger, and atomic swap with
   rollback — designed to fail closed.
5. **Capability-honest translation.** A tool-agnostic IR (`agents-md`,
   `rule`, `skill`, `command`, `plugin-reference`, `mcp-server-entry`) is
   translated by each adapter; per-target capability reports make
   lossy translations visible.

## Supported tools

Adapters translate the tool-agnostic IR into each tool's native files. Four
adapters ship bundled today; the rest are planned, and the `Planned (tier)`
label records the support tier each will land at.

| Tool | Status |
|------|--------|
| Claude Code | ✅ Bundled |
| Cursor | ✅ Bundled |
| Codex CLI | ✅ Bundled |
| Pi (`@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent`) | ✅ Bundled (agents-md; skill & command planned) |
| Antigravity CLI (replaces Gemini CLI, retired 2026-06-18) | Planned (supported) |
| Windsurf | Planned (experimental) |
| LM Studio | Planned (experimental) |

## Requirements

- Go 1.25+
- Git (shelled out for network operations)

## Build

```bash
go build ./cmd/agent-sync
```

## Test

```bash
go test -race ./...
golangci-lint run
```

## Project layout

See [`docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md`](docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md)
for the complete repo layout. The short version:

```
cmd/agent-sync/    CLI entry point
internal/          All implementation packages; no external API yet
docs/              Requirements, plans, specs
```

## Contributing

See [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) and [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) for guidance when
working on `agent-sync` itself (including from inside a coding agent). These
files are **in-repo agent guidance for contributors**, not output of the
sync pipeline.

## License

Licensed under the [Apache License 2.0](LICENSE). See [`NOTICE`](NOTICE) for
attribution requirements.

# Changelog

All notable changes to `agent-sync` are documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

While the project is pre-1.0, minor version bumps may include breaking changes;
the adapter wire protocol follows its own "freeze the frame, grow capabilities"
compatibility policy documented in `docs/spec/adapter-protocol-v1.md`.

## [0.5.0] - 2026-07-03

### Added

- **Skills now carry real descriptions.** Emitted `SKILL.md` files open with a
  YAML frontmatter block (`name:` plus `description:`) at byte 0, so Claude
  Code and other Agent-Skills consumers list the authored description instead
  of falling back to the managed-header comment. Author `description:` in your
  canonical `skills/<id>/SKILL.md` frontmatter (a new recognized IR frontmatter
  field); a skill with no description still emits — with a deterministic
  placeholder — and raises a degraded warning so the gap is visible. The
  claude, codex, and pi adapters render the block through one shared helper so
  co-emitted skills stay byte-identical. **Compatibility:** canonical repos that
  adopt `description:` require agent-sync ≥ 0.5.0 — older binaries reject the
  unknown frontmatter key.
- **`agent-sync update`** moves the canonical pin forward safely. It fetches the
  remote, resolves the manifest's `ref` (or the remote default), shows the
  old→new SHAs plus a commit change-summary, then re-pins `commit` +
  `trusted_sha` and re-syncs — all under one workspace run lock so a concurrent

50	- No cross-workspace sync, `--cascade`, or ancestor-sync in v1 (origin: Scope Boundaries). Target tools do global/user/project layering at runtime.
51	- No runtime daemon; watch + hooks only (origin: R4, R9).
52	- No in-process Go `plugin` package; adapters are subprocesses with a JSON contract (see Key Technical Decisions).
53	- No on-the-wire encryption, signed-commit enforcement, or sigstore attestation verification in v1 (design hook only).
54	
55	### Deferred to Separate Tasks
56	
57	- Supported-tier `antigravity` adapter and experimental-tier `windsurf`, `lmstudio`, `roo` adapters: separate PRs after primary adapters land. Their capability matrices are planning items per origin Deferred list. **Divergence (resolved, 2026-07-02):** the origin doc (R3) names the supported-tier target `gemini`, but Google retired Gemini CLI on 2026-06-18 in favor of Antigravity CLI, which reads the same `GEMINI.md` overlay alongside `AGENTS.md`. The deferred supported-tier adapter is therefore `antigravity`. The `GEMINI.md` root overlay keeps its filename-derived node id `gemini` but scopes to the `antigravity` target (target renamed `gemini` → `antigravity` in PR #36; spec updated in `docs/spec/ir-v1.md`). The origin brainstorm stays unedited as a dated historical record.
58	- Central adapter registry: defer past v1; discovery stays GitHub-topic-based (`aienvs-adapter`).
59	- `--cascade` / ancestor sync: explicitly out of scope.
60	- Symlink cycle detection beyond a bounded hop limit, and Windows-junction deep handling: origin Deferred; v1 ships with a bounded walk as the safety net.
61	
62	## Context & Research
63	
64	### Relevant Code and Patterns

- No cross-workspace sync, `--cascade`, or ancestor-sync in v1 (origin: Scope Boundaries). Target tools do global/user/project layering at runtime.
- No runtime daemon; watch + hooks only (origin: R4, R9).
- No in-process Go `plugin` package; adapters are subprocesses with a JSON contract (see Key Technical Decisions).
- No on-the-wire encryption, signed-commit enforcement, or sigstore attestation verification in v1 (design hook only).

### Deferred to Separate Tasks

- Supported-tier `antigravity` adapter and experimental-tier `windsurf`, `lmstudio`, `roo` adapters: separate PRs after primary adapters land. Their capability matrices are planning items per origin Deferred list. **Divergence (resolved, 2026-07-02):** the origin doc (R3) names the supported-tier target `gemini`, but Google retired Gemini CLI on 2026-06-18 in favor of Antigravity CLI, which reads the same `GEMINI.md` overlay alongside `AGENTS.md`. The deferred supported-tier adapter is therefore `antigravity`. The `GEMINI.md` root overlay keeps its filename-derived node id `gemini` but scopes to the `antigravity` target (target renamed `gemini` → `antigravity` in PR #36; spec updated in `docs/spec/ir-v1.md`). The origin brainstorm stays unedited as a dated historical record.
- Central adapter registry: defer past v1; discovery stays GitHub-topic-based (`aienvs-adapter`).
- `--cascade` / ancestor sync: explicitly out of scope.
- Symlink cycle detection beyond a bounded hop limit, and Windows-junction deep handling: origin Deferred; v1 ships with a bounded walk as the safety net.

### Relevant Code and Patterns

input.new_string:
## [Unreleased]

### Added

- **Antigravity adapter (bundled, full parity).** agent-sync now syncs to
  [Google Antigravity](https://antigravity.google) 2.0 (IDE + CLI), which
  replaced the retired Gemini CLI and reads the same `GEMINI.md` overlay. The
  adapter supports every IR kind except `plugin-reference`: `agents-md` →
  `GEMINI.md` managed section (not `AGENTS.md`, to avoid colliding with the
  codex/pi adapters), `rule` → `.agent/rules/agent-sync/`, `command` →
  `.agent/workflows/agent-sync/`, `skill` → the shared `.agents/skills/` tree,
  and `mcp-server-entry` → `.agents/mcp_config.json`. It faithfully reproduces
  Antigravity's own `.agent` (rules/workflows) vs `.agents` (skills/mcp)
  directory split. `agents-md`, `skill`, and `mcp-server-entry` are scope-aware
  (`sync --user` targets `~/.gemini/`); `rule` and `command` have no Antigravity
  user-global home and are reported as inert at user scope. See
  [`docs/adapters/antigravity.md`](docs/adapters/antigravity.md).

## [0.5.0] - 2026-07-03

Their capability matrices are planning items per origin Deferred list. **Divergence (resolved, 2026-07-02):** the origin doc (R3) names the supported-tier target `gemini`, but Google retired Gemini CLI on 2026-06-18 in favor of Antigravity CLI, which reads the same `GEMINI.md` overlay alongside `AGENTS.md`. content.subject: U1: Scaffold antigravity package (manifest + lifecycle)
content.description: bundled.go: Bundled(), run(), manifest, adapterName=antigravity, reservedPrefix=.agent, lifecycle wiring. Mirror claude/bundled.go.

content.subject: U2: Capabilities (conceptKinds + capabilities.yaml)
content.description: Full parity: agents-md/rule/skill/command/mcp supported, plugin-reference unsupported. capabilitiesForWire (scope-invariant), declaredOutputs (scope-aware). Parity test.

content.subject: U3: resolvePathSet + agents-md/mcp tool-owned emitters
content.description: emit_tool_owned.go: scope-aware pathSet, emitAgentsMD (GEMINI.md markdown section), emitMCPServerEntry (mcp_config.json JSON pointer, 2-stage validation). No sidecar.

content.subject: U4: rule/command/skill subdir emitters
content.description: emit_reserved.go + header.go: rule->.agent/rules/agent-sync, command->.agent/workflows/agent-sync (owned-subdir), skill->.agents/skills/agent-sync-<id> (shared-subdir). Mirror claude.

content.subject: U5: emit dispatch + plugin-reference warning
content.description: emit.go + emit_unsupported.go: handleEmit, dispatchNode, irNode wire shape, dedup/sort, nodeTargetsAntigravity, plugin-reference OpWarning. Drop sidecar from emitState.

content.subject: U6: test fixtures + emit tests
content.description: testdata/ir/*.json fixtures + emit_test.go/capabilities_test.go/header_test.go/bundled_test.go. Assert OpRecord lists at both scopes.

content.subject: U7: register adapter + coverage entries
content.description: setup.go bundledAdapters() += antigravity. coverage.go: nativeAtDirectory[antigravity]={agents-md}, nonNativeAtUser[antigravity]={rule,command}. Update any adapter-count test.

content.subject: U8: docs + roadmap update
content.description: docs/adapters/antigravity.md, README table (Four->Five, planned->supported), CHANGELOG, roadmap plan note. Final gate: go vet && go test -race && golangci-lint run.

built

  agent-sync syncs AI-agent configuration for multiple tools (Claude Code, Cursor, and more) from a single Git-backed   
  canonical manifest.                                                                                                   
         
  USAGE  
         
    agent-sync [command] [--flags]  
            
  COMMANDS  
            
    adapter [command]     Run adapter-focused developer workflows
    completion [command]  Generate the autocompletion script for the specified shell
    help [command]        Help about any command
    hooks [command]       Install or remove git hooks that run sync after pulls
    init [--flags]        Create a workspace manifest (.agent-sync.yaml)
    status                Show workspace, manifest, and per-target sync state
    sync [--flags]        Sync target tools from the canonical manifest
    trust [command]       Manage the two-tier trust store
    update [--flags]      Fetch new upstream commits and re-pin the canonical source
    validate [--flags]    Dry-Run the sync and report drift without writing anything
    watch [--flags]       Re-Sync automatically when the manifest or source changes
         
  FLAGS  
         
    -h --help             Help for agent-sync
    --log-level           Log level: debug, info, warn, error (info)
    --non-interactive     Never prompt; fail fast on a missing required value
    --offline             Forbid all network operations
    --output              Output format: text or json (default: text on a TTY, json when piped)
    -v --version          Version for agent-sync

input.command:
git commit --quiet -F - <<'EOF'
feat(adapter): add full-parity Antigravity bundled adapter

Add the bundled `antigravity` adapter for Google Antigravity 2.0 (IDE +
CLI, sharing config under ~/.gemini/), which replaced the retired Gemini
CLI and reads the same GEMINI.md overlay. The IR side was already wired
in PR #36 (GEMINI.md -> antigravity target); this adds the adapter
package, registration, coverage entries, and docs.

Full parity: agents-md, rule, skill, command, mcp-server-entry supported;
plugin-reference declines with a warning. The adapter faithfully
reproduces Antigravity's own directory-name split — rules/workflows under
.agent/ (singular, Windsurf lineage), skills/mcp under .agents/ (plural,
cross-tool convention):

  agents-md        -> GEMINI.md managed section (not AGENTS.md, which the
                      codex/pi adapters own)
  rule             -> .agent/rules/agent-sync/<id>.md
  command          -> .agent/workflows/agent-sync/<id>.md
  skill            -> .agents/skills/agent-sync-<id>/ (shared tree)
  mcp-server-entry -> .agents/mcp_config.json (/mcpServers/agentsync_<id>)

agents-md, skill, and mcp are scope-aware (sync --user targets
~/.gemini/); rule and command have no Antigravity user-global home and are
recorded in coverage.nonNativeAtUser as inert at user scope.

Verified via unit + integration tests and a real `agent-sync sync` e2e
observing the emitted .agent/ paths and GEMINI.md overlay. Gates:
go vet, go test -race, golangci-lint all clean.

Plan: docs/plans/2026-07-03-001-feat-antigravity-adapter-plan.md
Docs: docs/adapters/antigravity.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Claude-Session: https://claude.ai/code/REDACTED
EOF
git log --oneline -1

2	name: unit-16-restart-path
3	description: Where agent-sync resumes — v0.5.0 released 2026-07-03 (skill descriptions + agent-sync update command, zero-emit hint, Antigravity retarget; canonical description: needs ≥0.5.0); next candidates are pi command, --break-lock, Antigravity CLI adapter (Gemini CLI retired 2026-06-18); resume from latest docs/plans|handoffs/

8	---
9	
10	The agent-sync MVP is **done and shipped** — Unit 16 (CLI command tree + sync
11	orchestration engine) and the full Phase E landed long ago. As of 2026-06-10 the
12	product has three working bundled adapters (Claude, Cursor, Codex), production
13	hardening (Apache-2.0 license, threat model, goreleaser release packaging, an
14	enforced 80% CI coverage floor), and an end-to-end `init → sync → status →
15	validate` loop verified against real git + real filesystem.
16	
17	**v0.4.0 released 2026-07-02** (tag `v0.4.0`, stamp commit `7224ee9`): everything
18	below through PR #35 is now in a published release — pi adapter, scope-aware
19	`sync --user` (all 4 adapters), ADV-1 co-ownership + run-lock hardening, and
20	user-scope Cursor rule composition. Release workflow green, binary `--version`
21	verified. Next open items are the "Next candidates" list below, items 2–4
22	(pi `command`, `--break-lock`, Antigravity CLI adapter).
23	
24	**MERGED (2026-07-02): PR #38** (squash `b91321a` on main) — branch `feat/skill-descriptions-upstream-update`,
25	plan `docs/plans/2026-07-02-001-feat-skill-descriptions-upstream-update-plan.md`.
26	Two workstreams, 7 units (U1–U7), all committed, full `-race` gate + lint green.
27	**W1 skill descriptions:** `description` is now an authorable IR frontmatter field
28	(`internal/ir/kinds.go`,`types.go`); emitted `SKILL.md` is frontmatter-first
29	(`name`+`description` at byte 0) via a shared `internal/adapter/bundled/skillmeta`
30	helper (codex/pi byte-identical); managed headers interpolate real provenance
31	(`Source: <url>@<sha12>`, replacing the never-plumbed `{source-url}@{short-sha}`);
32	description-less skills emit-with-a-degraded-warning (capability-lie-gate safe).
33	**W2 update:** new `agent-sync update` (`internal/cli/cmd_update.go`) —
34	fast-forward-only re-pin (`IsAncestor(oldPin,newSHA)`) + gate (SHAs+ref+change
35	summary) + atomic `WriteResolvedSHA` + sync, all under one run lock via new
36	additive `engine.Options.RunLockHeld` (avoids the double-acquire deadlock).
37	Exit codes: 4 = needs `--accept-update`/`--accept-rewritten-history`, 6 = pin
38	moved but sync failed. Wire additions (`description`,`source_url`,`source_commit`
39	on node + InitializeParams) are additive/omitempty; SourceURL is always the
40	`cache.Canonicalize` form (no credential leak). Composed Cursor rules carry
41	per-node provenance (resolved open question). **Caveat:** ce-code-review was
42	spend-limit-degraded to an inline audit (subagents kept hitting the monthly
43	limit) — a full multi-persona pass before merge is prudent on this data-loss-
44	critical surface (engine lock seam + manifest mutation). See
45	[[spend-limit-degrades-review]]. Merged via --squash --admin (branch-protection
46	BLOCKED needs admin bypass, same as #36/#37; darwin/amd64 stall + CodeRabbit
47	queue were the only non-green checks — both non-blocking). Resolved 2 gemini
48	`t.Cleanup`-over-`defer` threads pre-merge (94a0481). **The Unreleased CHANGELOG
49	now holds three shipped-but-unreleased changes: #36 Antigravity retarget, #37
50	zero-emit hint, #38 skill descriptions + update — next `docs: stamp CHANGELOG
51	0.5.0` + tag `v0.5.0` release cuts them all** (note: canonical repos adopting
52	`description:` require ≥0.5.0). Remaining roadmap: pi `command`, `--break-lock`,
53	Antigravity CLI adapter.
54	
55	**To resume: read `docs/handoffs/2026-07-01-001-hierarchy-composition-handoff.md`**
56	— the current authoritative restart doc (state + the next task, hierarchy
57	composition, fully scoped). Plans run through 2026-07-01.
58	
59	**Latest state (2026-06-30):** All three bundled adapters are now scope-aware for
60	`sync --user`. PR #30 (merged, `378792f`) made the Claude adapter target
61	`~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` + `~/.claude.json`. PR #31 (**open**, branch
62	`fix/cursor-codex-scope-paths`) extends it to Cursor + Codex: Codex agents-md →
63	`~/.codex/AGENTS.md`; Cursor MCP → `~/.cursor/mcp.json` (no sidecar) with
64	rule/agents-md skipped at user scope (Cursor has no user-global file home for
65	them — confirmed against official docs 2026-06-30) and surfaced via
66	`internal/coverage` user-scope gap warnings. Plan:
67	`docs/plans/2026-06-30-001-fix-cursor-codex-scope-paths-plan.md`. See
68	[[claude-user-scope-gap]] (now resolved).
69	
70	**Pi adapter (Unit 11.5) — shipped across PRs (2026-06-30/07-01):**
71	- **PR #32 (MERGED, 1c62e5b):** pi adapter, agents-md only, scope-aware
72	  (`~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md` remap). mcp unsupported by design (no-MCP),
73	  rule/plugin unsupported. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-06-30-002-...`.
74	- **PR #33 (MERGED, 0cddd9e):** the **ADV-1 fix** + pi **skill**. Shared-subdir
75	  drift (`ScanDriftUnion`) + orphan + validate/`planTarget` reasoning now union
76	  all `req.Targets` ledgers so codex+pi co-own `.agents/skills/agent-sync-<id>`.
77	  4-persona ce-code-review (NO data loss) + resolved 10 PR threads (validate

79	  All 4 bundled adapters (claude/cursor/codex/pi) now coexist; pi supports
80	  agents-md + skill.
81	
82	- **PR #34 (MERGED, 8327474):** PR3, ADV-1 hardening.
83	  Per-workspace run lock (`.agent-sync/state/.sync.lock`, `internal/locks/runlock.go`)
84	  held across `engine.Sync`, closing the cross-process cross-delete race; +
85	  `warnOrphanLedgers` (non-destructive dropped-target warning; reclaim = future
86	  `unmanage`). 2-persona review found + fixed 2 P1s: run-lock contention now
87	  yields a clean StatusBlocked summary (not a hard error) so `--post-merge` still
88	  exits 0 (Options.RunLockTimeout, hook sets 3s); `warnOrphanLedgers` no longer
89	  mis-fires on capability-report.json. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-07-01-001-...`.

---
name: unit-16-restart-path
description: Where agent-sync resumes — v0.5.0 released 2026-07-03 (skill descriptions + agent-sync update command, zero-emit hint, Antigravity retarget; canonical description: needs ≥0.5.0); next candidates are pi command, --break-lock, Antigravity CLI adapter (Gemini CLI retired 2026-06-18); resume from latest docs/plans|handoffs/
metadata:
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 9d510688-0061-4983-885f-983207f8530f
---

The agent-sync MVP is **done and shipped** — Unit 16 (CLI command tree + sync
orchestration engine) and the full Phase E landed long ago. As of 2026-06-10 the
product has three working bundled adapters (Claude, Cursor, Codex), production
hardening (Apache-2.0 license, threat model, goreleaser release packaging, an
enforced 80% CI coverage floor), and an end-to-end `init → sync → status →
validate` loop verified against real git + real filesystem.

**v0.4.0 released 2026-07-02** (tag `v0.4.0`, stamp commit `7224ee9`): everything
below through PR #35 is now in a published release — pi adapter, scope-aware
`sync --user` (all 4 adapters), ADV-1 co-ownership + run-lock hardening, and
user-scope Cursor rule composition. Release workflow green, binary `--version`
verified. Next open items are the "Next candidates" list below, items 2–4
(pi `command`, `--break-lock`, Antigravity CLI adapter).

**MERGED (2026-07-02): PR #38** (squash `b91321a` on main) — branch `feat/skill-descriptions-upstream-update`,
plan `docs/plans/2026-07-02-001-feat-skill-descriptions-upstream-update-plan.md`.
Two workstreams, 7 units (U1–U7), all committed, full `-race` gate + lint green.
**W1 skill descriptions:** `description` is now an authorable IR frontmatter field
(`internal/ir/kinds.go`,`types.go`); emitted `SKILL.md` is frontmatter-first
(`name`+`description` at byte 0) via a shared `internal/adapter/bundled/skillmeta`
helper (codex/pi byte-identical); managed headers interpolate real provenance
(`Source: <url>@<sha12>`, replacing the never-plumbed `{source-url}@{short-sha}`);
description-less skills emit-with-a-degraded-warning (capability-lie-gate safe).
**W2 update:** new `agent-sync update` (`internal/cli/cmd_update.go`) —
fast-forward-only re-pin (`IsAncestor(oldPin,newSHA)`) + gate (SHAs+ref+change
summary) + atomic `WriteResolvedSHA` + sync, all under one run lock via new
additive `engine.Options.RunLockHeld` (avoids the double-acquire deadlock).
Exit codes: 4 = needs `--accept-update`/`--accept-rewritten-history`, 6 = pin
moved but sync failed. Wire additions (`description`,`source_url`,`source_commit`
on node + InitializeParams) are additive/omitempty; SourceURL is always the
`cache.Canonicalize` form (no credential leak). Composed Cursor rules carry
per-node provenance (resolved open question). **Caveat:** ce-code-review was
spend-limit-degraded to an inline audit (subagents kept hitting the monthly
limit) — a full multi-persona pass before merge is prudent on this data-loss-
critical surface (engine lock seam + manifest mutation). See
[[spend-limit-degrades-review]]. Merged via --squash --admin (branch-protection
BLOCKED needs admin bypass, same as #36/#37; darwin/amd64 stall + CodeRabbit
queue were the only non-green checks — both non-blocking). Resolved 2 gemini
`t.Cleanup`-over-`defer` threads pre-merge (94a0481). **The Unreleased CHANGELOG
now holds three shipped-but-unreleased changes: #36 Antigravity retarget, #37
zero-emit hint, #38 skill descriptions + update — next `docs: stamp CHANGELOG
0.5.0` + tag `v0.5.0` release cuts them all** (note: canonical repos adopting
`description:` require ≥0.5.0). Remaining roadmap: pi `command`, `--break-lock`,
Antigravity CLI adapter.

**To resume: read `docs/handoffs/2026-07-01-001-hierarchy-composition-handoff.md`**
— the current authoritative restart doc (state + the next task, hierarchy
composition, fully scoped). Plans run through 2026-07-01.

**Latest state (2026-06-30):** All three bundled adapters are now scope-aware for
`sync --user`. PR #30 (merged, `378792f`) made the Claude adapter target
`~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` + `~/.claude.json`. PR #31 (**open**, branch
`fix/cursor-codex-scope-paths`) extends it to Cursor + Codex: Codex agents-md →
`~/.codex/AGENTS.md`; Cursor MCP → `~/.cursor/mcp.json` (no sidecar) with
rule/agents-md skipped at user scope (Cursor has no user-global file home for
them — confirmed against official docs 2026-06-30) and surfaced via
`internal/coverage` user-scope gap warnings. Plan:
`docs/plans/2026-06-30-001-fix-cursor-codex-scope-paths-plan.md`. See
[[claude-user-scope-gap]] (now resolved).

**Pi adapter (Unit 11.5) — shipped across PRs (2026-06-30/07-01):**
- **PR #32 (MERGED, 1c62e5b):** pi adapter, agents-md only, scope-aware
  (`~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md` remap). mcp unsupported by design (no-MCP),
  rule/plugin unsupported. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-06-30-002-...`.
- **PR #33 (MERGED, 0cddd9e):** the **ADV-1 fix** + pi **skill**. Shared-subdir
  drift (`ScanDriftUnion`) + orphan + validate/`planTarget` reasoning now union
  all `req.Targets` ledgers so codex+pi co-own `.agents/skills/agent-sync-<id>`.
  4-persona ce-code-review (NO data loss) + resolved 10 PR threads (validate
  mirror, asset prefix-collision guard). Plan: `docs/plans/2026-06-30-003-...`.
  All 4 bundled adapters (claude/cursor/codex/pi) now coexist; pi supports
  agents-md + skill.

- **PR #34 (MERGED, 8327474):** PR3, ADV-1 hardening.
  Per-workspace run lock (`.agent-sync/state/.sync.lock`, `internal/locks/runlock.go`)
  held across `engine.Sync`, closing the cross-process cross-delete race; +
  `warnOrphanLedgers` (non-destructive dropped-target warning; reclaim = future
  `unmanage`). 2-persona review found + fixed 2 P1s: run-lock contention now
  yields a clean StatusBlocked summary (not a hard error) so `--post-merge` still
  exits 0 (Options.RunLockTimeout, hook sets 3s); `warnOrphanLedgers` no longer
  mis-fires on capability-report.json. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-07-01-001-...`.

**Next candidates (in order):**
1. **hierarchy composition** (MERGED 2026-07-01, PR #35 = `fd32998` on main;
   squash+--admin, branch deleted) — merge user-scope rule
   layer into project `.cursor/rules/` so global Cursor rules take effect
   (deferred from PR #31). **Go/no-go gate RESOLVED 2026-07-01:** recency-weighted
   research confirms Cursor still has NO native user-global rules file (User Rules
   are cloud/UI-only per Cursor staff Nov'25+Jan'26; `~/.cursor/rules/` exists on
   disk but is NOT reliably read; v3.9 "Customize" is UI-only). So composition is
   required — it does NOT collapse to a remap. Precedence Team>Project>User ⇒
   **project rules shadow user rules on id collision**. Seam: CLI-layer node
   injection in `runHierarchySync` (fold user `rule` nodes into project
   engine.Request.Nodes before engine.Sync); ownership falls out via project
   ledger orphan-reclaim. Engine untouched. **Plan written + deepened
   (5-persona ce-doc-review):**
   `docs/plans/2026-07-01-002-feat-hierarchy-composition-plan.md`. Decision:
   opt-in via manifest `compose.cursor-rules-from-user` (not automatic). Review
   verified the provenance-opaque bet is CLEAN (no .Provenance/.BlobSHA in
   engine/sync; ledger Entry = {Path, SHA256-of-bytes}) → U1 spike low-risk.
   Review-added guards folded in: D8 project sync stays hermetic (best-effort;
   never fetch a URL user source, never fail the run on user-materialize
   failure); D3 warns per shadowed id (flat id namespace → coincidental collision
   silently drops user rule); opt-out needs a re-sync to reclaim files;
   --workspace path doesn't compose (known limitation); Rule-of-Three (inline,
   no compose.go/userScopeOf helper). **SHIPPED to PR #35** (11 commits, U1–U6).
   8-persona ce-code-review fixed 2 P1s (empty-Targets leak → pin composed nodes
   to [cursor]; composeActive suppressing warning on soft-no-op → gate on
   len>0) + P3 (cursorRuleIDsOf). Residual P1 (transient user-source failure
   orphan-reclaims composed rules — because .cursor/rules/agent-sync is an
   OWNED SUBDIR swapped wholesale, NOT orphan-deleted per-file) resolved by
   **deferring the whole cursor sync when composeUserRules fails** (drop cursor
   from req.Targets that run; subdir left intact; project cursor edits wait for
   next good sync — accepted tradeoff). Key gotcha for future work: owned-subdir
   swap means "retain from orphan-delete" hooks DON'T protect files; only the
   staged desired-set survives. **Post-PR review round (Codex P1/P2, merged in
   #35):** composition now runs via a shared `applyCursorComposition` called from
   BOTH `runHierarchySync` AND `prepareEngine` — so `validate`, `watch`, and
   `sync --workspace` all compose too (else validate false-flags composed rules as
   WouldDelete drift, and watch/--workspace delete them). `hierarchy.UserScope(home)`
   added. Composition is now consistent across every command that builds a project
   request. Follow-ups still open: wire composition awareness elsewhere if a new
   command builds project requests; the [[capability-lie-gate-scope]] invariants.
2. **pi `command`** — needs file-leaf stage/swap for owned files in the shared
   flat `.pi/prompts/` dir (`sync.Stage` currently `MkdirAll`s a dir leaf only).
3. **`--break-lock` CLI flag** — wire AcquireOpts.BreakLock for both locks (not
   yet surfaced; recovery today = end the holder pid). ledger GC = `unmanage` (Unit 24).
4. Antigravity CLI adapter (was "Gemini adapter" — Google retired Gemini CLI
   2026-06-18 in favor of Antigravity CLI; enterprise Gemini Code Assist
   licenses keep Gemini CLI access. Antigravity CLI reads `AGENTS.md` and
   `GEMINI.md` (backward compat) plus `.antigravity.md` (preferred, wins over
   GEMINI.md); global rules live under `~/.gemini/` still. Adapter design
   should target Antigravity, not Gemini).

See [[capability-lie-gate-scope]].

**Immediate state (updated 2026-06-15):** PR #26 **open** —
dogfooding-readiness fix pass on branch `fix/dogfooding-readiness`. Fixed two
flagship-path bugs found by building the binary and running init→sync→validate:
(1) the **claude adapter double-wrapped agent-sync markers** in CLAUDE.md so
`sync --target claude` hard-failed (codex/cursor were fixed earlier; claude was
not) — now emits inner body like the others; (2) **`validate` falsely reported

on-disk; idempotent merge → no false drift, uniform across markdown/JSON/TOML),
plus a CRLF idempotency fix in `renderManagedBlock` (normalize managed-body
newlines to the file's style). Added a quickstart + `examples/canonical/`. Plan:
`docs/plans/2026-06-15-001-fix-dogfooding-readiness-plan.md`. CI green except the
chronic `test (darwin/amd64)` macos-13 hang (its arm64 twin + windows/amd64
passed). Deferred follow-ups in PR #26 body: shared-helper dedup

Next: merge #26, then resume v1.0.0-gate work.

**Prior state (2026-06-11):** PR #24 (codex +
shared-subdir fix) merged, and PR #25 — the full `aienvs`/`aienv` → `agent-sync`
rename (breaking on-disk + wire format: manifest `.agent-sync.yaml`, state dir

Dated historical docs under `docs/{plans,brainstorms,handoffs,solutions}/` keep

**Known dormant risk:** ADV-1 — two adapters sharing one shared parent

The data-loss-critical area is the swap/ownership model: `internal/engine/target.go`

content.text:
All 8 units complete, committed, verified, and memory updated. Here's where things stand.

## Done — Antigravity full-parity adapter shipped (local)

**Branch:** `feat/antigravity-adapter` · **Commit:** `b06b2b8` (not yet pushed)

**What "v2" turned out to be:** Antigravity 2.0 (IDE + CLI, config under `~/.gemini/`), which replaced the retired Gemini CLI and reads the same `GEMINI.md` overlay the codebase already routes to via PR #36.

**The load-bearing finding, faithfully implemented:** Antigravity's own inconsistent directory naming — `.agent/` (singular) for rules/workflows, `.agents/` (plural) for skills/mcp — reproduced rather than normalized (a normalized path would be inert).

| IR kind | Emits to | Scope-aware? |
|---|---|---|
| agents-md | `GEMINI.md` managed section (not AGENTS.md — avoids codex/pi collision) | ✅ → `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md` |
| rule | `.agent/rules/agent-sync/` | project-only (inert at user, coverage warns) |
| command | `.agent/workflows/agent-sync/` | project-only (inert at user, coverage warns) |
| skill | `.agents/skills/` (shared w/ codex/pi) | ✅ → `~/.gemini/skills/` |
| mcp-server-entry | `.agents/mcp_config.json` (`/mcpServers/agentsync_<id>`) | ✅ → `~/.gemini/config/` |
| plugin-reference | — (declines with warning) | — |

**Verification (evidence, not assertion):**
- `go vet ./...` clean · `go test -race ./...` all green · `golangci-lint run` 0 issues
- **Real `agent-sync sync` e2e**: drove the actual binary against a canonical repo → observed `.agent/rules/agent-sync/no-fri.md`, `.agent/workflows/agent-sync/fmt.md`, and `GEMINI.md` with a proper `agent-sync:begin id=gemini` managed section. Exit 0.

**14 files:** new `internal/adapter/bundled/antigravity/` package (9 source + 3 test + 6 fixtures), registration in `setup.go`, coverage entries + tests, `docs/adapters/antigravity.md`, README/CHANGELOG, roadmap + plan docs.

**One correction to prior project notes:** the earlier memory predicted a `.antigravity.md` file would win over GEMINI.md. Fresh research and PR #36 both settle on GEMINI.md; no current source mentions `.antigravity.md`. I built to GEMINI.md and corrected the memory.

## Next step needs your call

The repo's workflow is PR-per-adapter (#36/#37/#38). Pushing + opening a PR is outward-facing, so I'll confirm first.

input.command:
gh pr create --base main --head feat/antigravity-adapter --title "feat(adapter): add full-parity Antigravity bundled adapter" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## What

Adds the bundled `antigravity` adapter for **Google Antigravity 2.0** (IDE + CLI, sharing config under `~/.gemini/`), which replaced the retired Gemini CLI (2026-06-18) and reads the same `GEMINI.md` overlay. The IR side was already wired by PR #36 (`GEMINI.md → antigravity` target); this PR adds the adapter package, registration, coverage entries, docs, and tests.

Fifth bundled adapter (joins claude, cursor, codex, pi).

## Concept → destination

Antigravity is internally inconsistent about its config directory name, and the adapter **reproduces this faithfully** — a normalized path would be inert (Antigravity wouldn't read it). Rules/workflows use `.agent/` (singular, Windsurf lineage); skills/mcp use `.agents/` (plural, cross-tool convention).

| IR kind | Status | Project | User scope (`sync --user`) |
|---|---|---|---|
| `agents-md` | ✅ | `GEMINI.md` managed section | `.gemini/GEMINI.md` |
| `rule` | ✅ | `.agent/rules/agent-sync/<id>.md` | *(no user-global home — inert; coverage warns)* |
| `command` | ✅ | `.agent/workflows/agent-sync/<id>.md` | *(no user-global home — inert; coverage warns)* |
| `skill` | ✅ | `.agents/skills/agent-sync-<id>/` (shared w/ codex/pi) | `.gemini/skills/…` |
| `mcp-server-entry` | ✅ | `.agents/mcp_config.json` (`/mcpServers/agentsync_<id>`) | `.gemini/config/mcp_config.json` |
| `plugin-reference` | ❌ | warning, unsupported | — |

## Key decisions

- **GEMINI.md only, not AGENTS.md** — `AGENTS.md` is owned by codex/pi; a second writer at the same scope would collide. `GEMINI.md` is Antigravity-specific and highest-precedence.
- **No MCP sidecar** — `mcp_config.json` is a shared tool-owned merge target (like claude's `~/.claude.json` at user scope), not an agent-sync-owned strict-JSON file.
- **`rule`/`command` project-only** — Antigravity documents no user-global directory for them; recorded in `coverage.nonNativeAtUser` so `sync --user` reports them inert honestly.
- Path research is sourced from Google codelabs + Gemini API docs + Google DevRel posts (the official docs site is a client-rendered SPA). Any path drift is localized to `resolvePathSet`/`declaredOutputs`/subdir constants. See `docs/adapters/antigravity.md`.

## Verification

- `go vet ./...` clean · `go test -race ./...` all green · `golangci-lint run` 0 issues
- **Real `agent-sync sync` e2e**: drove the binary against a canonical repo → observed `.agent/rules/agent-sync/no-fri.md`, `.agent/workflows/agent-sync/fmt.md`, and `GEMINI.md` with a proper `agent-sync:begin id=gemini` managed section (exit 0).

## Docs

- Plan: `docs/plans/2026-07-03-001-feat-antigravity-adapter-plan.md`
- Adapter doc: `docs/adapters/antigravity.md`
- CHANGELOG (Unreleased) + README adapter table (Four → Five) + roadmap plan updated

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF

content.text:
Done. **Antigravity support is shipped as PR #39.**

## Summary

Full-parity `antigravity` bundled adapter for **Google Antigravity 2.0** (the "v2" you flagged — IDE + CLI sharing config under `~/.gemini/`), built on accurate current docs and the codebase's existing PR #36 IR wiring.

- **Research → decision:** Antigravity reads `GEMINI.md` (highest precedence) — the target the codebase already routes to. I confirmed against Google's own codelabs, the Gemini API docs, and Google DevRel posts, and surfaced the one genuinely surprising fact: Antigravity's `.agent/` (rules/workflows, singular) vs `.agents/` (skills/mcp, plural) split, which the adapter reproduces faithfully.
- **Implementation:** 8 units, TDD, mirroring the `claude`/`pi` adapters — new package + registration + coverage + docs + 3 test files + 6 fixtures.
- **Verified with evidence:** `go vet` / `go test -race` / `golangci-lint` all clean, plus a real `agent-sync sync` end-to-end that emitted the actual `.agent/…` files and the `GEMINI.md` managed section.
- One stale prior prediction (`.antigravity.md`) corrected — no current source supports it; GEMINI.md is the settled target.

The PR is open and awaiting CI. Note the recurring `test (darwin/amd64)` runner stall is infra, not a code signal — don't fix-loop on it if it hangs.
